text:
# 401(k) Matching Policy

**Effective Date:** April 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees eligible for benefits

## 1. Employer Match

Acme Corp matches 50% of employee contributions up to 6% of salary. This results in a maximum employer contribution of 3% of salary.

**Example:** An employee earning $100,000 per year who contributes 6% ($6,000) receives a 50% match of $3,000. Contributions above 6% of salary are not matched.

| Employee Contribution (% of salary) | Employer Match (% of salary) |
|---|---|
| 1% | 0.5% |
| 2% | 1.0% |
| 3% | 1.5% |
| 4% | 2.0% |
| 5% | 2.5% |
| 6% or more | 3.0% (maximum) |

## 2. Eligibility

All benefits-eligible employees may participate in the 401(k) plan from their first day of employment. Enrollment is available through the benefits portal at any time; there is no requirement to wait for open enrollment.

Employees are automatically enrolled at a 3% contribution rate within 30 days of their start date unless they opt out or select a different rate.

## 3. Vesting Schedule

Employer matching contributions vest over a 4-year schedule:

| Years of Service | Vested Percentage |
|---|---|
| Less than 1 year | 0% |
| 1 year | 25% |
| 2 years | 50% |
| 3 years | 75% |
| 4 years | 100% |

Employee contributions are always 100% vested. Only the employer match is subject to the vesting schedule. Unvested employer contributions are forfeited upon separation from the company.

## 4. Contribution Limits

Employee contributions are subject to IRS annual limits. For 2025, the limit is $23,500 for employees under 50 and $31,000 for employees 50 and older (includes the $7,500 catch-up contribution). Limits are updated annually; Payroll will notify employees of changes each January.

Contributions may be made on a pre-tax or Roth (after-tax) basis. The employer match is always made on a pre-tax basis regardless of the employee's election.

## 5. Fund Options

The plan offers a selection of index funds, target-date funds, and a self-directed brokerage window. A full list of available funds, expense ratios, and historical performance is available on the benefits portal. Acme Corp does not provide investment advice; employees are encouraged to consult a financial advisor.

## 6. Loans and Hardship Withdrawals

Participants may borrow from their vested 401(k) balance up to the lesser of $50,000 or 50% of the vested balance. Loan terms do not exceed 5 years (or 15 years for a primary residence purchase). One outstanding loan is permitted at a time.

Hardship withdrawals are available for qualifying events as defined by IRS regulations. Hardship withdrawals are subject to income tax and may incur a 10% early withdrawal penalty for participants under 59½.

## 7. Policy Governance

The 401(k) plan is administered by Human Resources in partnership with Fidelity Investments as the plan recordkeeper. Questions should be directed to benefits@acmecorp.com or ext. 4200.
title: 401(k) Matching Policy

# Acceptable Use Policy

**Document ID:** ITSEC-006
**Effective Date:** November 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees and contractors

## 1. Scope

This policy applies to all individuals who access Acme Corp systems, networks, or data, including full-time employees, part-time employees, temporary workers, interns, and contractors. The same standards apply regardless of employment classification. References to "users" throughout this document include all of these groups.

## 2. Permitted Use

Company-provided equipment, systems, and network access are provided for business purposes. Users may:

- Access company systems to perform their assigned job duties.
- Use company email and messaging for business communications.
- Use company devices for limited personal use (e.g., personal email during breaks, brief web browsing) provided it does not interfere with work responsibilities, consume excessive bandwidth, or violate any other provision of this policy.
- Install software from the approved software catalog. Software not in the catalog requires an IT exception request.

## 3. Prohibited Activities

The following activities are prohibited on company systems, networks, and devices:

- Accessing, downloading, or distributing illegal content.
- Installing unauthorized software, including peer-to-peer file sharing applications, cryptocurrency mining software, or unapproved browser extensions.
- Attempting to bypass security controls, disable endpoint protection, or tamper with audit logging.
- Using company resources for personal commercial purposes (side businesses, freelance work, personal advertising).
- Sharing credentials, using another person's account, or allowing others to use your access.
- Connecting unauthorized devices to the corporate network without IT approval.
- Storing Confidential or Restricted data on personal cloud services (Google Drive personal, Dropbox personal, iCloud).
- Sending Confidential or Restricted data to personal email addresses.
- Excessive personal use that degrades system performance or bandwidth for other users.

## 4. Monitoring Disclosure

Acme Corp monitors the use of company systems, networks, and devices. Monitoring includes but is not limited to:

- Network traffic analysis (bandwidth usage, destination domains, protocol analysis).
- Email scanning for malware and data loss prevention (DLP) rule violations.
- Endpoint activity logging (application usage, file access on company devices).
- Access logs for company applications and cloud services.

Users should have no expectation of privacy when using company-provided systems and devices. Monitoring is conducted for security, compliance, and operational purposes. Monitoring data is reviewed by the Information Security team and is not used for general performance evaluation. Refer to the Employee Monitoring Policy for the full scope and governance of monitoring activities.

## 5. Internet and Social Media

- Web browsing for personal use during breaks is permitted. Accessing sites that are illegal, offensive, or that violate other company policies (harassment, discrimination) is prohibited at all times.
- Employees should not represent themselves as speaking on behalf of Acme Corp on social media unless authorized by the Communications team.
- Posting Confidential or Restricted company information on any public forum, social media platform, or personal blog is prohibited.

## 6. Mobile and BYOD

Personal devices used to access company systems must be enrolled in the Mobile Device Management (MDM) program per the BYOD Policy. Unenrolled personal devices may not connect to the corporate network or access company data.

## 7. Remote Work

Users accessing company systems remotely must use the Acme Corp VPN. See the VPN Access Guide for setup and connection instructions. All provisions of this policy apply equally when working remotely.

## 8. Consequences of Violation

Violations of this policy may result in:

- Temporary or permanent revocation of system access.
- Disciplinary action up to and including termination (for employees).
- Immediate contract termination (for contractors).
- Referral to law enforcement where illegal activity is suspected.

The severity of the response is determined by the nature and intent of the violation, whether it is a first or repeated offense, and the impact on company systems, data, or other users.

## 9. Reporting Violations

Users who become aware of a violation of this policy should report it to the Information Security team at security@acmecorp.com or through the anonymous ethics hotline at 1-800-555-0142.

## 10. Policy Governance

This policy is reviewed annually by the Information Security team. Questions should be directed to security@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 2.0 — Last revised November 1, 2023*
title: Acceptable Use Policy

text:
# Access Control Policy

**Document ID:** ITSEC-003
**Effective Date:** June 15, 2024
**Approved by:** VP of Engineering
**Classification:** Confidential

## 1. Purpose

This policy defines the access controls that govern how employees, contractors, and systems access Acme Corp's production systems, internal applications, and sensitive data. Access controls ensure that only authorized individuals can access resources necessary for their role, reducing the risk of unauthorized disclosure, modification, or destruction of company and customer information.

## 2. Principles

### 2.1 Least Privilege

All access is granted on the principle of least privilege. Users receive the minimum level of access necessary to perform their assigned duties. Elevated access for temporary tasks (e.g., incident investigation, migration) must be requested through the IT portal and is automatically revoked after the approved duration.

### 2.2 Separation of Duties

Critical functions must be divided among multiple individuals to prevent any single person from having end-to-end control over a sensitive process. Examples include:

- The person who writes code must not be the same person who deploys it to production.
- The person who creates a vendor payment must not be the same person who approves it.
- Database administrators must not have the ability to modify audit logs.

### 2.3 Need-to-Know

Access to Confidential and Restricted data requires a documented business justification. Access controls for these classifications are enforced at the application, database, and network levels.

## 3. Role-Based Access Controls (RBAC)

### 3.1 Role Definitions

Acme Corp uses role-based access controls to manage permissions. Standard roles include:

| Role | Access Level | Example Systems |
|---|---|---|
| Viewer | Read-only access to non-sensitive data | Wiki, internal dashboards, team documents |
| Contributor | Read and write access to team resources | Code repositories, project boards, shared drives |
| Admin | Full control over a specific system or application | System configuration, user management, data exports |
| Super Admin | Cross-system administrative access | Identity provider, secrets management, production infrastructure |

### 3.2 Role Assignment

Roles are assigned based on job function and level. The default access for a new employee is determined by their department and role during onboarding. Additional access beyond the default requires a formal access request.

| Job Function | Default Role |
|---|---|
| Individual contributor | Viewer + Contributor (own team resources) |
| People manager | Viewer + Contributor + Admin (team-specific systems) |
| IT operations | Contributor + Admin (infrastructure systems) |
| Security engineer | Contributor + Admin (security tools) + Viewer (production logs) |
| Executive (L7+) | Viewer (company-wide dashboards) |

### 3.3 Privileged Access

Privileged accounts (Admin and Super Admin) are subject to additional access controls:

- Multi-factor authentication is required for every session.
- Sessions are logged with full command capture for production systems.
- Privileged access is reviewed monthly (not quarterly) by the InfoSec team.
- Just-in-time (JIT) access is used where possible: privileged access is granted for a defined window and automatically revoked.

## 4. Access Lifecycle

### 4.1 Provisioning

New access is provisioned through the following process:

1. Employee or manager submits a request via the IT portal, specifying the system, the role requested, and the business justification.
2. The system owner reviews and approves or denies within 2 business days.
3. IT provisions the access and confirms with the requestor.
4. The access grant is logged in the identity management system with the approval chain.

### 4.2 Modification

When an employee's role changes (promotion, lateral move, team transfer), the following occurs:

- The employee's previous access is reviewed within 5 business days of the effective date.
- Access that is no longer required is revoked.
- New access appropriate to the new role is provisioned through the standard request process.

Managers are responsible for initiating the access review when a direct report changes roles.

### 4.3 Revocation

Access is revoked:

- Immediately upon involuntary termination.
- At the end of the business day on the employee's last day for voluntary resignation.
- At the end of the contract period for contractors (per the Contractor Access Policy).
- Within 1 business day when a system owner determines that access is no longer needed.

### 4.4 Quarterly Access Reviews

System owners conduct quarterly access reviews for all systems handling Confidential or Restricted data. The review process:

1. IT generates a report of all active accounts and their roles for each system.
2. The system owner reviews each account and confirms or revokes access.
3. Accounts that have not been used in 90 days are flagged for removal.
4. The completed review is submitted to the InfoSec team and retained for audit purposes.

Quarterly access reviews are a SOC 2 control and must be completed within 15 business days of the quarter end.

## 5. Access Controls for Specific Environments

### 5.1 Production Environment

Access to production systems is restricted to:

- On-call engineers (read access to logs and metrics; write access limited to incident remediation).
- Database administrators (through the approved database proxy with query logging).
- The automated deployment pipeline (no interactive human access).

Direct SSH access to production servers is prohibited. All production access is through the bastion host with MFA and full session recording.

### 5.2 Customer Data

Access to customer data requires:

- A documented business justification reviewed by the system owner.
- Completion of the data handling training for the relevant classification level.
- For Restricted data (PII, PHI): approval from the Data Protection Officer in addition to the system owner.

Customer data access is logged and audited monthly by the InfoSec team.

### 5.3 Third-Party Access

Third-party vendors and partners who require access to Acme Corp systems must:

- Sign a data processing agreement (see the Data Processing Agreement Template).
- Access only through the vendor VPN gateway with MFA.
- Be sponsored by an Acme Corp employee who serves as the access owner.
- Have access scoped to the minimum necessary for the contracted work.

Third-party access is reviewed monthly by the sponsoring employee and IT.

## 6. Monitoring and Enforcement

All access control events (grants, revocations, denials, escalations) are logged in the identity management system and forwarded to the SIEM for monitoring. The InfoSec team investigates anomalies including:

- Access requests for systems outside the employee's normal scope.
- Repeated access denials.
- Accounts with excessive permissions relative to their role.
- Dormant accounts with active access.

Violations of this policy are reported to the employee's manager and Human Resources. Repeated or willful violations may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination.

## 7. Policy Governance

This policy is reviewed annually by the Information Security team and approved by the VP of Engineering. Questions should be directed to security@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 2.0 — Last revised June 15, 2024*
title: Access Control Policy

Acme Corp — Admin Console Guide
  



Admin Console Guide

Last Updated: January 15, 2025
Applies to: Account owners and users with the Admin role



  1. Accessing the Admin Console

  The admin console is available at app.acmecorp.com/admin. Only users with the Owner or Admin role can access the console. Admins see a gear icon in the left sidebar navigation that links directly to it.





  2. User Management


  2.1 Viewing Users

  The Users tab displays all users on the account with their name, email, role, status (active, invited, suspended), last login date, and MFA enrollment status.


  2.2 Inviting Users

  Click Invite User, enter the email address, select a role (Admin, Member, or Viewer), and optionally assign the user to one or more workspaces. The invitee receives an email with a link that expires after 7 days.


  2.3 Changing Roles

  Click a user's name, then Edit Role. Role changes take effect immediately. Downgrading an Admin to Member revokes access to the admin console. Only the account Owner can promote a user to Admin.


  2.4 Suspending and Removing Users

  Suspend: Blocks the user from logging in while preserving their data and workspace memberships. Useful when an employee is on leave or under investigation. Suspended users do not count toward the seat limit for billing.

  Remove: Permanently removes the user from the account. Their records are reassigned to the account Owner unless a different assignee is specified. Removal cannot be undone; the user must be re-invited to regain access.





  3. SSO Configuration


  3.1 Supported Protocols

  
    ProtocolProviders Tested

    SAML 2.0Okta, Azure AD, OneLogin, PingFederate

    OIDCOkta, Google Workspace, Auth0

  

  3.2 Setup Steps

  
    Navigate to Admin > Security > Single Sign-On.

    Select SAML 2.0 or OIDC.

    Enter the metadata URL or upload the metadata XML from your identity provider.

    Map identity provider attributes to Acme Corp fields (email, first name, last name, department).

    Enable SSO. Optionally, enforce SSO (disabling password-based login for all non-Owner users).

    Test the connection using the Test SSO button before enforcing.

  

  3.3 SCIM Provisioning

  Enterprise customers can enable SCIM 2.0 provisioning to automatically create, update, and deactivate Acme Corp user accounts from their identity provider. Configure SCIM under Admin > Security > Provisioning. A SCIM bearer token is generated during setup — store it securely in your identity provider's configuration.





  4. Audit Log


  4.1 Viewing the Audit Log

  The audit log is available under Admin > Audit Log. It records all significant actions performed by users and the system:

  
    Event CategoryExamples

    AuthenticationLogin, logout, failed login, MFA challenge, password reset

    User managementUser invited, role changed, user suspended, user removed

    Record operationsRecord created, updated, deleted, restored, exported

    ConfigurationSSO configured, API key generated, webhook registered, integration connected

    BillingPlan changed, seats added, payment processed, refund issued

  

  4.2 Filtering and Exporting

  Use the filters at the top of the log to narrow results by date range, user, event category, or specific action. The audit log is searchable by keyword. Export the filtered results as CSV for compliance reporting.


  4.3 Retention

  Audit log entries are retained for 90 days on Starter, Pro, and Premium plans. Enterprise customers have extended retention of 1 year in searchable storage plus 6 years in archive, accessible on request. See the Audit Log Retention policy for details.





  5. API Key Management


  5.1 Generating Keys

  Navigate to Admin > API > Keys and click Generate New Key. Assign a name, select a permission scope (read, read-write, or admin), and optionally restrict the key to specific IP addresses. The key is displayed only once — copy it immediately. See the API Authentication documentation for usage details.


  5.2 Managing Keys

  The API Keys list shows all active keys with their name, scope, creation date, last used date, and creator. You can revoke a key at any time by clicking Revoke. Revoked keys stop working immediately.


  5.3 Usage Monitoring

  Click a key's name to see usage statistics: requests per day, error rate, and top endpoints by volume. Set up alerts for unusual patterns (spike in 401 errors, requests from new IP addresses) under Admin > API > Alerts.





  6. Support

  For admin console issues, contact support@acmecorp.com or use the in-app chat (Pro and above, business hours).
title: Admin Console Guide

text:
# AI Ethics Policy

**Effective Date:** November 15, 2024
**Approved by:** VP of Engineering (Sarah Chen), Chief Compliance Officer (Jordan Patel)
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees and contractors involved in the development, deployment, or operation of AI and machine learning systems

---

## 1. Scope

This policy governs all AI and machine learning systems developed or deployed by Acme Corp, including:

- ML-powered features in the Acme Corp platform (anomaly detection, semantic search, natural language query).
- Internal tools that use machine learning (spam filtering, support ticket routing, lead scoring).
- Third-party AI services integrated into Acme Corp products or operations.

## 2. Bias Testing

All ML models that produce outputs visible to customers or that influence decisions affecting individuals must undergo bias testing before deployment and on an ongoing basis:

- **Pre-deployment:** Bias evaluation across protected demographic categories (where applicable data is available) using fairness metrics appropriate to the use case (demographic parity, equalized odds, or calibration).
- **Post-deployment:** Quarterly bias monitoring for production models. If a model's fairness metrics degrade beyond the threshold established during pre-deployment evaluation, the model is flagged for review and retraining.
- **Documentation:** Bias test results, methodology, and thresholds are documented in the model card for each production model. Model cards are reviewed by the ML Platform team lead and the Data Protection Officer.

Models that cannot be meaningfully tested for bias (e.g., anomaly detection on infrastructure metrics with no demographic dimension) are exempt from demographic bias testing but must still undergo accuracy and error-rate analysis.

## 3. Explainability

Acme Corp requires that AI systems provide explanations appropriate to the stakes of their output:

| Risk Level | Explainability Requirement | Examples |
|---|---|---|
| Low | Model documentation and aggregate performance metrics available | Search ranking, content recommendations, auto-tagging |
| Medium | Per-prediction explanation available to internal operators on request | Support ticket routing, lead scoring, anomaly flagging |
| High | Per-prediction explanation provided to the affected individual automatically | Any model output used in access control decisions, compliance scoring, or fraud detection |

"Explanation" means a description of the key factors that influenced the model's output, in terms understandable to a non-technical audience. Feature importance scores, attention visualizations, or similar technical artifacts are acceptable for internal operators but must be translated into plain language for end-user-facing explanations.

## 4. Human-in-the-Loop

For high-stakes decisions — defined as decisions that may result in denial of service, account termination, financial penalty, or adverse action against an individual — a human must review and approve the decision before it takes effect.

Specifically:

- No customer account may be terminated or suspended solely on the basis of an automated model output.
- No employee may be subjected to disciplinary action based solely on automated monitoring or scoring (see also the Employee Monitoring Policy, Section 7).
- No access to a service or feature may be permanently revoked by an automated system without human review.
- Fraud detection models may temporarily freeze transactions pending human review, but the freeze must be reviewed within 4 hours during business hours or 12 hours outside business hours.

Automated systems may take temporary protective actions (rate limiting, temporary holds, alert generation) without human approval, provided that a human reviews the action within the timeframes above.

## 5. Prohibited Use Cases

Acme Corp prohibits the use of AI for the following purposes:

- **Mass surveillance:** Using AI to monitor or profile individuals at scale without specific, documented justification and legal basis.
- **Social scoring:** Assigning scores to individuals based on social behavior, personal characteristics, or predicted personality traits.
- **Manipulative design:** Using AI to exploit psychological vulnerabilities to influence user behavior against their interests.
- **Autonomous weapons:** Development of or contribution to autonomous weapons systems.
- **Biometric categorization:** Inferring sensitive attributes (race, political opinion, sexual orientation, religious belief) from biometric data.

Employees who believe an AI use case may fall into a prohibited category should raise the concern with the ML Platform team lead or the Data Protection Officer before proceeding.

## 6. Third-Party AI Services

Before integrating a third-party AI service into any Acme Corp product or workflow:

1. The Engineering team must complete an AI vendor assessment covering the vendor's bias testing practices, data handling, and model documentation.
2. The Data Protection Officer reviews the data flows to ensure compliance with the Data Processing Agreement and applicable privacy regulations.
3. The third-party service must not process customer data for the vendor's own model training unless explicitly authorized by the customer.

## 7. Governance and Review

This policy is reviewed semi-annually by the AI Ethics Review Board, which consists of:

- VP of Engineering (chair).
- Data Protection Officer.
- One representative from Product.
- One representative from Legal.
- One external advisor (rotated annually).

The Review Board evaluates new AI use cases, reviews bias test results, and assesses whether the policy remains aligned with evolving regulations and industry best practices.

## 8. Contact

Questions about this policy should be directed to ml-ethics@acmecorp.com or the Data Protection Officer at dpo@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.0 — Last revised November 15, 2024*
title: AI Ethics Policy

Acme Corp Annual Report
Fiscal Year 2024 (January 1 – December 31, 2024)
Letter from the CEO
To our shareholders, customers, and employees:
Fiscal year 2024 was a year of disciplined growth for Acme Corp. We grew revenue by 15% year-
over-year while expanding our customer base, deepening platform adoption, and investing in
the infrastructure that will carry us through the next phase of scale.
Three themes defined the year. First, our enterprise segment proved that the product-market
fit we built in the mid-market translates to larger organizations. Enterprise annual recurring
revenue grew 28%, driven by multi-year contracts and expanded use cases. Second, our in-
vestment in the ML platform — funded by the Q3 budget reallocation approved by VP of En-
gineering Sarah Chen — positioned us to deliver AI-powered features that our customers are
already adopting. Third, we maintained our commitment to security and compliance, renewing
our ISO 27001 certification and completing our SOC 2 Type II audit with zero critical findings.
We enter 2025 with strong momentum, a healthy balance sheet, and a clear roadmap. I am
grateful to our employees for their execution and to our customers for their trust.
— David Kim, Chief Executive Officer

pansion and cross-sell of the Compliance Suite to existing Core Platform customers.
VP of
Engineering Sarah Chen approved the $2.1M Q3 budget reallocation to the ML platform team
4


on August 15, 2024, accelerating the feature store and model serving buildout. APAC revenue
crossed $3M in a single quarter for the first time.

Product Development Highlights
Core Platform
• Migrated the search infrastructure to PostgreSQL with HNSW vector indexing, achieving
sub-50ms p99 latency for semantic search queries. The migration was completed in Q2
with zero downtime.
• Launched a new API gateway with per-customer rate limiting and usage-based metering,
supporting the shift toward consumption-based pricing for select enterprise accounts.
• Reduced average page load time from 1.8s to 0.9s through frontend optimization and
edge caching.
Data & Analytics
• Shipped ML-powered anomaly detection for time-series data, adopted by 43 enterprise
customers in the first 6 months.
• Introduced natural language query capability (beta), allowing non-technical users to ex-
plore data using conversational questions.
• Expanded the pre-built dashboard library from 24 to 51 templates covering SaaS metrics,
financial reporting, and operational KPIs.
Compliance Suite
• Achieved HIPAA compliance, enabling onboarding of healthcare customers. 12 healthcare
organizations signed in Q3–Q4.
• Launched automated evidence collection for SOC 2 audits, reducing audit preparation
time for customers by an estimated 60%.
5


• Added GDPR data subject access request (DSAR) automation, processing requests in an
average of 2.3 hours vs. the industry average of 15 hours.
Customer Case Studies
HealthFirst Medical Group
HealthFirst, a network of 34 clinics across the Pacific Northwest, adopted the Compliance Suite
in Q2 2024 to automate HIPAA compliance workflows. Prior to Acme Corp, HealthFirst’s com-
pliance team spent an estimated 120 hours per quarter on manual evidence collection for
audits. After deployment, automated evidence collection reduced this to 18 hours per quarter
— an 85% reduction. HealthFirst expanded their contract in Q4 to include the Data & Analytics
product for patient outcome reporting.
“Acme Corp’s platform cut our audit prep time from weeks to days.
The automated HIPAA
controls give us confidence that we’re always audit-ready, not just scrambling before an as-
sessment.” — Director of Compliance, HealthFirst Medical Group
Pinnacle Financial Services
Pinnacle, a mid-market wealth management firm with $8B in assets under management, de-
ployed the Core Platform and Data & Analytics suite to consolidate client reporting.
The
ML-powered anomaly detection feature identified three instances of unusual trading patterns
within the first month, flagging them for compliance review before they became regulatory
issues. Pinnacle’s annual contract value grew from $180K to $340K over FY 2024 through seat
expansion and product cross-sell.
Verdant Manufacturing
Verdant, a specialty chemicals manufacturer with 1,200 employees across 6 facilities, adopted
the Core Platform to centralize operational documentation. The platform replaced a legacy
SharePoint deployment and reduced document search time from an average of 4.2 minutes
to 12 seconds. Verdant is currently piloting the Compliance Suite for SOX internal controls
documentation.
Technology and Infrastructure
Platform Architecture
Acme Corp’s platform is deployed on a multi-region cloud architecture with primary infrastruc-
ture in US-East and secondary infrastructure in EU-West (deployed Q2 2024). The platform
serves approximately 14,000 daily active users with an average API response time of 87ms
(p50) and 210ms (p99).

The Information Security team expanded to 8 full-time members in FY 2024.
Key security
achievements:
• Zero data breaches in FY 2024.
• Completed 2 external penetration tests and 4 internal penetration tests with all findings
remediated within SLA.
• Deployed a new SIEM platform, reducing mean time to detect (MTTD) security anomalies
from 4.2 hours to 38 minutes.
• Achieved a patch compliance rate of 98.4% (percentage of critical and high vulnerabilities
remediated within SLA).
• Conducted 4 incident response tabletop exercises with cross-functional teams.
Risk Factors
The following risk factors could materially affect Acme Corp’s business, financial condition, and
results of operations:
Competition. The SaaS market is competitive and evolving. New entrants and established
vendors may offer features that are comparable to or exceed our own, potentially at lower
prices.
Customer concentration. Our top 10 customers represented 18% of total revenue in FY
2024. Loss of one or more of these customers could have a material impact.
Regulatory environment. Changes in data protection regulations (GDPR, HIPAA, emerging
state privacy laws) may require significant product investment or limit our ability to serve
certain markets.
Key personnel. Our success depends on the continued service of key employees, including
our executive team and senior engineers. Loss of key personnel could disrupt product devel-
opment and customer relationships.
Economic conditions. A prolonged economic downturn could reduce enterprise IT spending,
lengthen sales cycles, and increase customer churn.
Security. A significant security breach or data loss could damage our reputation, result in
regulatory penalties, and lead to customer losses. We invest in security (SOC 2, ISO 27001,
HIPAA) but no system is immune to attack.

API Authentication — Acme Corp Developer Docs
  



API Authentication

Base URL: https://api.acmecorp.com/v2
Last Updated: January 15, 2025


All requests to the Acme Corp API must be authenticated. This guide covers the two supported authentication methods: API keys and OAuth 2.0.



  1. API Key Authentication


  1.1 Generating an API Key

  Account administrators can generate API keys in the Acme Corp dashboard:

  
    Navigate to Settings > API > Keys.

    Click Generate New Key.

    Assign a descriptive name (e.g., "Production Backend", "CI/CD Pipeline").

    Select the permission scope (read-only, read-write, or admin).

    Copy the key immediately — it is displayed only once.

  
  
    Security: API keys are displayed only at the time of creation. Store them in a secrets manager (e.g., HashiCorp Vault, AWS Secrets Manager). Do not commit API keys to source code, store them in environment variables on shared systems, or send them via email or chat.
  


  1.2 Using an API Key

  Include the API key in the Authorization header of every request:

  GET /v2/data/records HTTP/1.1
Host: api.acmecorp.com
Authorization: Bearer acme_sk_live_7f3a9b2c...
  API keys use the prefix acme_sk_live_ for production keys and acme_sk_test_ for sandbox keys. Do not use test keys against the production API or vice versa.


  1.3 Key Rotation

  API keys should be rotated regularly to reduce the risk of credential compromise. Acme Corp recommends rotating keys every 180 days.

  To rotate a key without downtime:

  
    Generate a new key in the dashboard.

    Update your application to use the new key.

    Verify that requests succeed with the new key.

    Revoke the old key in the dashboard.

  
  Both the old and new keys are active simultaneously until the old key is explicitly revoked, giving you time to deploy the change.


  1.4 Key Permissions

  
    ScopeAllowed Operations

    readGET requests only. Cannot create, update, or delete resources.

    read-writeGET, POST, PUT, PATCH, DELETE on resources owned by the account.

    adminAll operations, including user management, billing, and API key management.

  
  Follow the principle of least privilege: grant each key only the permissions it needs. CI/CD pipelines typically need read-write; monitoring tools typically need read.





  2. OAuth 2.0 Authentication

  OAuth 2.0 is the recommended authentication method for applications that act on behalf of a user (e.g., third-party integrations, partner apps). Acme Corp supports two OAuth 2.0 flows:


  2.1 Authorization Code Flow (Recommended)

  Use the Authorization Code flow for server-side applications that can securely store a client secret.

  
    
      Redirect the user to the authorization endpoint:
      GET https://auth.acmecorp.com/oauth/authorize?
  response_type=code&
  client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
  redirect_uri=https://yourapp.com/callback&
  scope=read+write&
  state=random_csrf_token
    

    User authenticates with their Acme Corp credentials and approves the requested scopes.

    
      Acme Corp redirects back to your redirect_uri with an authorization code:
      https://yourapp.com/callback?code=AUTH_CODE&state=random_csrf_token
    

    
      Exchange the code for tokens:
      POST https://auth.acmecorp.com/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=authorization_code&
code=AUTH_CODE&
client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET&
redirect_uri=https://yourapp.com/callback
    

    
      Receive the token response:
      {
  "access_token": "acme_at_8f2b...",
  "token_type": "Bearer",
  "expires_in": 3600,
  "refresh_token": "acme_rt_4c7d...",
  "scope": "read write"
}
    

  

  2.2 Client Credentials Flow

  Use the Client Credentials flow for machine-to-machine communication where no user context is needed (e.g., backend service calling the Acme Corp API).

  POST https://auth.acmecorp.com/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=client_credentials&
client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET&
scope=read
  The response contains an access token but no refresh token. Request a new token when the current one expires.


  2.3 Token Lifetimes

  
    Token TypeLifetimeRenewable

    Access token1 hourNo (request a new one using the refresh token)

    Refresh token30 daysYes (each use issues a new refresh token)

    Authorization code10 minutesNo (single use)

  

  2.4 Refreshing Tokens

  POST https://auth.acmecorp.com/oauth/token
Content-Type: application/x-www-form-urlencoded

grant_type=refresh_token&
refresh_token=acme_rt_4c7d...&
client_id=YOUR_CLIENT_ID&
client_secret=YOUR_CLIENT_SECRET
  Each refresh request returns a new access token and a new refresh token. The previous refresh token is invalidated (rotation). If a refresh token is used more than once, all tokens in the chain are revoked as a security precaution.


  2.5 Scopes

  
    ScopeDescription

    readRead access to the user's resources

    writeCreate and update resources

    deleteDelete resources

    adminAccount administration (user management, billing)

    offline_accessRequest a refresh token for long-lived access

  
  Request only the scopes your application needs. Users see the requested scopes during the authorization step and may decline if the request seems excessive.





  3. What Happens When Authentication Fails

  When a system fails to authenticate against the Acme Corp API, the server returns an HTTP error response with a JSON body describing the failure. The response depends on the type of authentication failure:


  3.1 Error Responses

  
    HTTP StatusError CodeDescriptionCommon Cause

    401 Unauthorizedinvalid_api_keyThe API key is missing, malformed, or has been revoked.Key was revoked or rotated; application is still using the old key.

    401 Unauthorizedexpired_tokenThe OAuth access token has expired.Token lifetime (1 hour) exceeded; application did not refresh.

    401 Unauthorizedinvalid_tokenThe OAuth token is malformed or has been revoked.Token was part of a revoked refresh chain; client stored a corrupted token.

    403 Forbiddeninsufficient_scopeThe authenticated token does not have the required scope for this endpoint.Token was issued with read scope but the request requires write.

    403 Forbiddenip_not_allowedThe request originates from an IP address not in the key's allowlist.IP allowlist configured on the key does not include the requesting server.

    429 Too Many Requestsrate_limitedThe API key or token has exceeded the rate limit.Too many requests in the time window. Check the Retry-After header.

  

  3.2 Example Error Response

  {
  "error": "invalid_api_key",
  "error_description": "The API key provided is not valid. It may have been revoked or rotated.",
  "status": 401,
  "documentation_url": "https://docs.acmecorp.com/api/auth#errors"
}

  3.3 Handling Authentication Failures

  Your application should handle authentication failures as follows:

  
    401 with expired_token: Attempt to refresh the token using the refresh token. If the refresh fails, redirect the user to re-authenticate.

    401 with invalid_api_key: Check that the key has not been revoked. Generate a new key if necessary. Do not retry with the same key.

    403 with insufficient_scope: Request a new token with the required scopes. This may require user re-authorization.

    429 rate_limited: Wait for the duration specified in the Retry-After response header before retrying. Implement exponential backoff for repeated 429 responses.

  
  
    Tip: Log all authentication errors with the x-request-id response header value. When contacting support, include this ID so we can trace the request through our systems.
  





  4. Security Best Practices

  
    Store API keys and client secrets in a secrets manager, never in source code or configuration files.

    Rotate API keys every 180 days.

    Use IP allowlists to restrict where API keys can be used from.

    Use the Authorization Code flow (not Client Credentials) when acting on behalf of a user, to ensure proper consent and scope.

    Request the minimum necessary scopes for each integration.

    Monitor API key usage in the dashboard under Settings > API > Usage. Set up alerts for unusual patterns (unexpected IP addresses, spike in 401 errors).

    Revoke keys and tokens immediately if you suspect a compromise.

  




  5. Rate Limits

  API rate limits are applied per API key or OAuth token:

  
    PlanRequests per MinuteConcurrent Connections

    Starter605

    Pro30020

    Enterprise1,000+Custom

  
  Rate limit status is returned in response headers: X-RateLimit-Limit, X-RateLimit-Remaining, and X-RateLimit-Reset (Unix timestamp).





  6. Support

  For authentication issues, contact api-support@acmecorp.com or visit docs.acmecorp.com/api.
title: API Authentication Documentation

text:
Acme Corp REST API Reference
  



Acme Corp REST API Reference

API Version: v2
Base URL: https://api.acmecorp.com/v2
Last Updated: January 15, 2025


This reference documents all available endpoints in the Acme Corp REST API. All requests must be authenticated using an API key or OAuth 2.0 token. See the API Authentication documentation for details.



  1. Overview


  
    1.1 Base URL and Versioning

    All API endpoints are served from https://api.acmecorp.com/v2. The version is included in the URL path. When a new major version is released, the previous version remains available for at least 12 months after the deprecation announcement.

    The current version (v2) was released in March 2024. The deprecated v1 API remains accessible until March 2025.

  


  
    1.2 Request Format

    All request bodies must be JSON-encoded with the Content-Type: application/json header. Query parameters are used for filtering, sorting, and pagination. Path parameters identify specific resources.

  


  
    1.3 Response Format

    All responses are JSON-encoded. Successful responses return a data field containing the result. Error responses return an error field with a machine-readable code and a human-readable description.

    {
  "data": { ... },
  "meta": {
    "request_id": "req_8f2b4a...",
    "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
}
    Every response includes a unique request_id in the meta object and in the X-Request-ID response header. Include this ID when contacting support.

  





  2. Authentication

  See API Authentication for full details. In summary:

  
    API key: Pass in the Authorization: Bearer acme_sk_live_... header.

    OAuth 2.0: Pass the access token in the Authorization: Bearer acme_at_... header.

  
  Unauthenticated requests receive a 401 Unauthorized response.





  3. Pagination


  
    3.1 Cursor-Based Pagination

    List endpoints return paginated results using cursor-based pagination. Each response includes a pagination object:

    {
  "data": [ ... ],
  "pagination": {
    "has_more": true,
    "next_cursor": "eyJpZCI6MTAwfQ==",
    "previous_cursor": null
  }
}
    To fetch the next page, pass the next_cursor value as the cursor query parameter:

    GET /v2/records?cursor=eyJpZCI6MTAwfQ==&limit=50
  


  
    3.2 Page Size

    Use the limit query parameter to control the number of results per page:

    
      ParameterDefaultMinimumMaximum

      limit251100

    
    Requesting a limit above 100 returns a 400 Bad Request error.

  


  
    3.3 Total Count

    List endpoints include a total_count field in the response when the include_count=true query parameter is passed. Counting adds latency for large collections; omit it when not needed.

  





  4. Rate Limits


  
    4.1 Limits by Plan

    
      PlanRequests per MinuteRequests per DayConcurrent Connections

      Starter6010,0005

      Pro300100,00020

      Enterprise1,000+CustomCustom

    
  


  
    4.2 Rate Limit Headers

    Every response includes rate limit information in headers:

    
      HeaderDescription

      X-RateLimit-LimitMaximum requests per minute for the current key/token

      X-RateLimit-RemainingRequests remaining in the current window

      X-RateLimit-ResetUnix timestamp when the window resets

    
  


  
    4.3 Handling 429 Responses

    When the rate limit is exceeded, the API returns 429 Too Many Requests with a Retry-After header (in seconds). Implement exponential backoff: wait the Retry-After duration, then double the wait on each consecutive 429 until the request succeeds.

  





  5. Records API


  
    5.1 List Records

    GET /v2/records

    Returns a paginated list of records in the authenticated account.

    Query Parameters

    
      ParameterTypeRequiredDescription

      limitintegerNoResults per page (default 25, max 100)

      cursorstringNoPagination cursor from a previous response

      sortstringNoSort field: created_at, updated_at, name (default: created_at)

      orderstringNoasc or desc (default: desc)

      statusstringNoFilter by status: active, archived, deleted

      created_afterISO 8601NoFilter records created after this timestamp

      created_beforeISO 8601NoFilter records created before this timestamp

      qstringNoFull-text search across record name and content

    
    Example Response

    {
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "rec_a1b2c3",
      "name": "Q3 2025 Revenue Summary",
      "status": "active",
      "created_at": "2025-01-10T14:30:00Z",
      "updated_at": "2025-01-12T09:15:00Z",
      "created_by": "user_x7y8z9",
      "tags": ["finance", "quarterly"]
    }
  ],
  "pagination": {
    "has_more": true,
    "next_cursor": "eyJpZCI6MTAxfQ==",
    "previous_cursor": null
  },
  "meta": {
    "request_id": "req_f3d2a1...",
    "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:30:00Z"
  }
}
  


  
    5.2 Get a Record

    GET /v2/records/{record_id}

    Returns a single record by ID, including its full content.

    Path Parameters

    
      ParameterTypeDescription

      record_idstringThe record's unique identifier (e.g., rec_a1b2c3)

    
    Response

    Returns the full record object including content (the record body), metadata, and attachments array.

    Returns 404 Not Found if the record does not exist or the authenticated user does not have access.

  


  
    5.3 Create a Record

    POST /v2/records

    Creates a new record. Requires write scope.

    Request Body

    
      FieldTypeRequiredDescription

      namestringYesRecord name (max 255 characters)

      contentstringYesRecord body (max 1 MB)

      tagsarray of stringsNoTags for categorization (max 20 tags, max 50 chars each)

      metadataobjectNoArbitrary key-value pairs (max 50 keys, max 500 chars per value)

    
    Example Request

    POST /v2/records HTTP/1.1
Host: api.acmecorp.com
Authorization: Bearer acme_sk_live_7f3a...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "name": "Meeting Notes — Product Review",
  "content": "Discussed roadmap priorities for Q2...",
  "tags": ["meetings", "product"],
  "metadata": {
    "department": "Product",
    "meeting_date": "2025-01-14"
  }
}
    Returns 201 Created with the full record object. Returns 422 Unprocessable Entity if required fields are missing or validation fails.

  


  
    5.4 Update a Record

    PATCH /v2/records/{record_id}

    Updates specific fields of an existing record. Only include fields you want to change. Requires write scope.

    Request Body

    Same fields as Create, all optional. Omitted fields are left unchanged. To clear a field, pass null.

    Returns 200 OK with the updated record. Returns 404 Not Found or 422 Unprocessable Entity as appropriate.

  


  
    5.5 Delete a Record

    DELETE /v2/records/{record_id}

    Soft-deletes a record. The record's status is set to deleted and it no longer appears in list responses by default. Deleted records can be restored within 30 days via a support request. After 30 days, the record and its content are permanently purged.

    Requires delete scope. Returns 204 No Content on success.

  





  6. Users API


  
    6.1 List Users

    GET /v2/users

    Returns a paginated list of users in the authenticated account. Requires admin scope.

    Query Parameters

    
      ParameterTypeRequiredDescription

      limitintegerNoResults per page (default 25, max 100)

      cursorstringNoPagination cursor

      rolestringNoFilter by role: owner, admin, member, viewer

      statusstringNoFilter by status: active, invited, suspended

    
  


  
    6.2 Get a User

    GET /v2/users/{user_id}

    Returns a single user profile. Requires admin scope or the requesting user's own user_id.

  


  
    6.3 Invite a User

    POST /v2/users/invite

    Sends an invitation email to a new user. Requires admin scope.

    Request Body

    
      FieldTypeRequiredDescription

      emailstringYesEmail address of the invitee

      rolestringYesadmin, member, or viewer

      teamsarray of stringsNoTeam IDs to add the user to upon acceptance

    
    Returns 201 Created. The invitation expires after 7 days. Returns 409 Conflict if the email is already associated with an active user on the account.

  


  
    6.4 Update a User

    PATCH /v2/users/{user_id}

    Updates a user's role or status. Requires admin scope. Cannot modify the account owner.

  


  
    6.5 Remove a User

    DELETE /v2/users/{user_id}

    Removes a user from the account. Their records are reassigned to the account owner unless a reassign_to parameter is specified. Requires admin scope. Returns 204 No Content.

  





  7. Search API


  
    7.1 Search Records

    POST /v2/search

    Full-text and semantic search across records in the authenticated account.

    Request Body

    
      FieldTypeRequiredDescription

      querystringYesSearch query (max 500 characters)

      modestringNokeyword, semantic, or hybrid (default: hybrid)

      limitintegerNoMaximum results (default 10, max 50)

      filtersobjectNoFilter by tags, created_after, created_before, status, metadata

    
    Example Request

    POST /v2/search HTTP/1.1
Host: api.acmecorp.com
Authorization: Bearer acme_at_8f2b...
Content-Type: application/json

{
  "query": "revenue forecast Q3",
  "mode": "hybrid",
  "limit": 5,
  "filters": {
    "tags": ["finance"],
    "created_after": "2025-01-01T00:00:00Z"
  }
}
    Example Response

    {
  "data": [
    {
      "id": "rec_a1b2c3",
      "name": "Q3 2025 Revenue Summary",
      "snippet": "...projected revenue of $38.2M for Q3, representing 16% YoY growth...",
      "score": 0.94,
      "highlights": ["revenue", "Q3"]
    }
  ],
  "meta": {
    "request_id": "req_b4c5d6...",
    "search_time_ms": 47,
    "total_results": 12,
    "timestamp": "2025-01-15T10:35:00Z"
  }
}
    Results are ranked by relevance score (0.0 to 1.0). In hybrid mode, scores combine keyword (BM25) and semantic (vector similarity) signals.

  





  8. Webhooks API


  
    8.1 Register a Webhook

    POST /v2/webhooks

    Request Body

    
      FieldTypeRequiredDescription

      urlstringYesHTTPS endpoint to receive events (must be publicly accessible)

      eventsarray of stringsYesEvents to subscribe to: record.created, record.updated, record.deleted, user.invited, user.removed

      secretstringNoShared secret for signature verification. Auto-generated if not provided.

    
    Returns 201 Created with the webhook object including the id and secret.

  


  
    8.2 Webhook Delivery

    Events are delivered as POST requests to the registered URL with a JSON body:

    {
  "id": "evt_m3n4o5",
  "type": "record.updated",
  "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:40:00Z",
  "data": {
    "record_id": "rec_a1b2c3",
    "changes": ["content", "tags"]
  }
}
    Each delivery includes an X-Acme-Signature header containing an HMAC-SHA256 signature of the request body using the webhook secret. Verify this signature to ensure the request is from Acme Corp.

    Retry policy: If your endpoint does not respond with a 2xx status within 10 seconds, the delivery is retried up to 5 times with exponential backoff (10s, 30s, 90s, 270s, 810s). After 5 failures, the webhook is marked as failing and an email notification is sent to the account owner.

  


  
    8.3 List Webhooks

    GET /v2/webhooks

    Returns all registered webhooks for the authenticated account. Requires admin scope.

  


  
    8.4 Delete a Webhook

    DELETE /v2/webhooks/{webhook_id}

    Removes a webhook subscription. Pending deliveries are cancelled. Returns 204 No Content.

  





  9. Response Codes

  
    CodeMeaningWhen You'll See It

    200OKSuccessful GET, PATCH requests

    201CreatedSuccessful POST requests that create a resource

    204No ContentSuccessful DELETE requests

    400Bad RequestMalformed request, invalid parameters, limit out of range

    401UnauthorizedMissing, invalid, or expired authentication credentials

    403ForbiddenAuthenticated but insufficient permissions for the requested operation

    404Not FoundResource does not exist or is not accessible to the authenticated user

    409ConflictResource already exists (e.g., duplicate invite)

    422Unprocessable EntityRequest is well-formed but fails validation (missing required fields, invalid values)

    429Too Many RequestsRate limit exceeded. Check Retry-After header.

    500Internal Server ErrorUnexpected server error. Include X-Request-ID when contacting support.

    503Service UnavailableAPI is temporarily unavailable (maintenance or overload). Retry with backoff.

  




  10. Changelog

  
    DateVersionChange

    2025-01-15v2.4Added hybrid search mode (default). Added metadata filter to search.

    2024-10-01v2.3Increased max limit from 50 to 100 for list endpoints.

    2024-07-15v2.2Added webhook signature verification (X-Acme-Signature).

    2024-04-01v2.1Added cursor-based pagination (replacing offset-based).

    2024-03-01v2.0Initial v2 release. Breaking changes from v1: new auth format, JSON-only responses, renamed endpoints.
title: Acme Corp REST API Reference

2024 Internal Compliance Audit — Findings and
Recommendations
2024 Internal Compliance Audit
Report Date: February 15, 2025 Audit Period: January 1 – December 31, 2024 Conducted
by: Internal Audit team, with oversight from Chief Compliance Officer Jordan Patel Classifica-
tion: Confidential
1. Executive Summary
The 2024 internal compliance audit reviewed six departments across 42 controls mapped to
SOC 2, ISO 27001, HIPAA, and internal policy requirements.
Of the 42 controls tested, 35
passed (83%), 5 had minor findings requiring remediation, and 2 had significant findings re-
quiring immediate action.

Overall, Acme Corp’s compliance posture improved from the 2023 audit (78% pass rate). Two

most minor findings.
Engineering, Finance, and IT maintained strong compliance.

3. Findings by Department
3.1 Engineering — All Controls Passed
All 10 controls passed without findings. Notably:
• Code review coverage: 100% of production deployments had at least one approved re-
view.
• Change management: All production changes were tracked through the CI/CD pipeline
with audit trail.
• SOC 2 evidence collection was automated during Q2, reducing manual effort by 60%.
No remediation required.
3.2 Finance — All Controls Passed
All 8 controls passed. Revenue recognition, SOX internal controls testing, and expense audit
sampling all met standards. The Finance team completed 100% of mandatory training on time.
No remediation required.
3.3 IT / Information Security — 1 Minor Finding
Minor Finding IT-01: Quarterly access review completion (Q3)
The Q3 quarterly access review for two systems (the analytics platform and the staging envi-
ronment) was completed 8 days past the 15-business-day deadline. Root cause: the system
owner was on leave and no delegate was assigned.
Remediation: Assign a backup reviewer for all systems before each quarterly review cycle.
IT has implemented this starting Q4 2024.
Status: Remediated.
3.4 Human Resources — 1 Minor Finding
Minor Finding HR-01: Harassment prevention training completion rate
Annual harassment prevention training was completed by 94% of employees by the February
28 deadline.
The remaining 6% (31 employees) completed training between March 1 and
March 22. Policy requires 100% completion by the deadline.
Remediation: HR will implement automated escalation at 7 days and 14 days past deadline,
including manager notification and system access suspension at 30 days (aligned with the
Mandatory Training Catalog non-compliance escalation).
Status: Remediation in progress. New escalation workflow launches Q1 2025.
3.5 Sales & Marketing — 1 Minor Finding, 1 Significant Finding
Minor Finding SM-01: Customer data used in marketing materials
Two case studies published in Q2 included customer revenue figures that had not been ap-
proved by the customer for external use. The data was accurate but its disclosure was not
authorized under the customer’s contract.
Remediation: Marketing must obtain written approval from the customer and Legal before
publishing any customer-specific data. A pre-publication checklist has been added to the mar-
keting workflow.
2


Status: Remediated.
Significant Finding SM-02: HIPAA training non-compliance
The Sales team expanded into healthcare vertical selling in Q2 2024. Seven sales represen-
tatives handling healthcare prospects had not completed HIPAA Basics training as required
by the Mandatory Training Catalog. Three of these representatives accessed customer demo
environments containing simulated PHI during product demonstrations.
Remediation required: 1. All sales representatives in the healthcare vertical must complete
HIPAA Basics training within 30 days of this report. 2. Sales management must verify HIPAA
training completion before assigning healthcare accounts. 3. Demo environments must not
contain PHI (real or simulated) until the representative’s training is confirmed.
Status: Open. Deadline: March 17, 2025.
3.6 Customer Success — 2 Minor Findings, 1 Significant Finding
Minor Finding CS-01: Customer data export handling
Two Customer Success Managers exported customer data to local laptops for analysis during
quarterly business reviews. The exports contained Confidential data that should have been
analyzed in the platform or through the approved BI tool, not downloaded locally.
Remediation: Customer Success must use the platform’s built-in analytics or the approved
BI tool for customer data analysis.
Local exports of Confidential or Restricted data require
Information Security approval per the Data Classification Policy.
Status: Remediated. Policy reminder issued to team.
Minor Finding CS-02: Data subject request response time
Three data subject access requests (DSARs) from EU customers were responded to in 38, 41,
and 45 days — exceeding the 30-day GDPR requirement. Root cause: the requests were han-
dled by individual CSMs rather than routed to the DPO team.
Remediation: All DSARs must be routed to dpo@acmecorp.com within 24 hours of receipt.
Customer Success is no longer authorized to handle DSARs independently.
Status: Remediated. Routing workflow implemented in Q4.
Significant Finding CS-03: Security awareness training completion
The Customer Success department had the lowest security awareness training completion rate
at 81% as of the March 31 deadline.
The department-wide rate remained below the 90%
threshold at the end of Q2 (87%). A remediation plan was submitted in July and the team
reached 98% by end of Q3.
Remediation required: 1. The Customer Success department head must submit a training
completion plan at the start of each year identifying at-risk team members and scheduling
training during Q1. 2. The department will be subject to monthly training compliance checks
(rather than quarterly) for the next 12 months.
Status: Monitoring. Elevated oversight through Q1 2026.
4. Cross-Department Themes
Three themes emerged across multiple departments:
3


1. Training completion gaps. Three of the seven findings related to training not being
completed on time (HR-01, SM-02, CS-03). Departments that failed training controls must
implement the escalation process defined in the Mandatory Training Catalog, including
system access suspension at 30 days past deadline.
2. Data handling outside approved channels. Two findings (SM-01, CS-01) involved data
being used or stored outside approved workflows. The Data Classification Policy training
should emphasize the prohibition on local exports of Confidential data.
3. DSAR processing ownership. Customer-facing teams should not process DSARs inde-
pendently. All data subject requests must flow through the DPO office.

Open and in-progress findings are tracked in the compliance management system. The Chief

6. Recommendations
1. Automate training compliance enforcement company-wide using the escalation frame-
work in the Mandatory Training Catalog (system access suspension at 30 days past dead-
line).
2. Conduct a mid-year training compliance check in July in addition to the annual deadline
assessment.
3. Implement a DLP (Data Loss Prevention) rule to flag exports of Confidential data to local
storage, with automatic notification to Information Security.
4. Require HIPAA training completion as a prerequisite for healthcare account assignment in
the CRM system.
5. Expand the internal audit scope in 2025 to include the Product and Legal departments
(not audited in 2024).
7. Next Steps
The 2025 internal compliance audit is scheduled for January–December 2025, with the report
due by February 2026. Scope will expand from 42 to approximately 50 controls to cover the
Product and Legal departments.
Questions about this report should be directed to compliance@acmecorp.com.

# Audit Log Retention

**Document ID:** ITSEC-009
**Effective Date:** January 1, 2024
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp systems that generate audit logs

## 1. Retention Schedule

Audit logs are retained in two tiers:

| Storage Tier | Duration | Access | SLA |
|---|---|---|---|
| Hot storage (indexed, searchable) | 1 year | Real-time query via the SIEM and log management platform | Sub-second search response |
| Cold storage (archived) | 6 years | Retrievable within 72 hours upon request to IT Operations | 72-hour retrieval SLA |

Total retention is 7 years from the date the log entry was created. This meets the requirements of SOC 2, HIPAA, and Acme Corp's general data retention policy of 7 years for business records.

## 2. What Is Logged

All systems handling Confidential or Restricted data must generate audit logs capturing:

| Event Category | Fields Captured |
|---|---|
| Authentication | Timestamp (UTC), user ID, source IP, authentication method, success/failure |
| Authorization | Timestamp, user ID, resource requested, permission checked, granted/denied |
| Data access | Timestamp, user ID, source IP, operation (read/write/delete), record or resource identifier |
| Administrative actions | Timestamp, admin user ID, action performed, target system/configuration, before/after values |
| System events | Timestamp, system identifier, event type (startup, shutdown, error, configuration change) |

Logs for Internal-tier systems must capture authentication and administrative actions at a minimum. Logging for Public-tier systems is optional.

## 3. Log Integrity

- Logs are written to append-only storage. Log entries cannot be modified or deleted by the system or users whose activity they record.
- Logs are forwarded from source systems to the centralized logging infrastructure within 60 seconds of generation.
- The logging infrastructure resides in the management network zone, isolated from production and corporate zones.
- Log integrity is verified daily via checksum comparison between source and centralized copies.

## 4. Access to Audit Logs

Access to audit logs is restricted:

| Role | Access |
|---|---|
| Information Security | Full read access for investigation and monitoring |
| IT Operations | Read access for operational troubleshooting (hot storage only) |
| Data Protection Officer | Read access for compliance audits |
| Legal | Access upon documented request tied to litigation hold or regulatory inquiry |
| System Owners | Read access to logs for their own systems (hot storage only) |

No role has the ability to modify or delete audit log entries.

## 5. Retention Justification

The 7-year total retention period is driven by:

- **SOC 2 Type II:** Requires evidence of continuous control operation; audit logs are primary evidence for access control and change management controls.
- **HIPAA:** Requires retention of security-related documentation for 6 years from the date of creation or the date it was last in effect.
- **Acme Corp Data Retention Policy:** General business records are retained for 7 years.
- **Incident investigation:** Historical logs are frequently needed for root cause analysis of security incidents with delayed detection.

## 6. Disposal

After 7 years, audit logs are permanently deleted using cryptographic erasure (destroying the encryption keys for the archived log volumes). Deletion is logged in the data disposal register and confirmed by the Information Security team.

## 7. Policy Governance

This policy is maintained by the Information Security team. Questions should be directed to security@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.1 — Last revised January 1, 2024*
title: Audit Log Retention

# Backup and Recovery Policy

**Document ID:** ITSEC-007
**Effective Date:** June 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp production systems, databases, and critical business applications

## 1. Recovery Objectives

| Objective | Target |
|---|---|
| Recovery Point Objective (RPO) | 1 hour — no more than 1 hour of data loss in a disaster scenario |
| Recovery Time Objective (RTO) | 4 hours — systems restored to operational state within 4 hours of a declared disaster |

These targets apply to all Tier 1 (production) and Tier 2 (business-critical) systems. Tier 3 (development, staging) systems have relaxed targets of RPO 24 hours / RTO 24 hours.

## 2. Backup Schedule

| System Tier | Backup Type | Frequency | Retention |
|---|---|---|---|
| Tier 1 (production databases) | Continuous WAL/binlog replication | Real-time | 30 days of point-in-time recovery |
| Tier 1 (production databases) | Full snapshot | Daily at 2:00 AM UTC | 90 days |
| Tier 2 (business apps, file storage) | Incremental | Every 6 hours | 30 days |
| Tier 2 (business apps, file storage) | Full snapshot | Weekly (Sunday 3:00 AM UTC) | 90 days |
| Tier 3 (dev, staging) | Full snapshot | Weekly | 14 days |

All backups are encrypted at rest using AES-256 per the Encryption Standards.

## 3. Backup Storage

- **Primary backups** are stored in a separate availability zone within the same cloud region as the source system.
- **Secondary backups** are replicated to a geographically separate region (minimum 500 miles from the primary region) within 1 hour of creation.
- Backup storage is access-controlled. Only the IT Operations team and designated database administrators have access. Access is logged and reviewed quarterly.

## 4. Recovery Testing

Recovery tests are conducted quarterly to validate that backups are usable and that RTO/RPO targets can be met.

Each quarterly test includes:

- Full restoration of at least one Tier 1 database from backup to an isolated recovery environment.
- Verification of data integrity (row counts, checksums, application-level validation).
- Measurement of actual recovery time against the 4-hour RTO target.
- Documentation of any issues encountered and remediation actions.

Test results are reported to the VP of Engineering and retained for audit purposes. Failed tests are treated as SEV3 incidents per the Incident Response Runbook.

## 5. Disaster Recovery

In the event of a regional outage or catastrophic failure:

1. The incident commander declares a disaster recovery event.
2. IT Operations initiates failover to the secondary region using the secondary backup set.
3. Application teams verify service health in the recovery environment.
4. DNS and load balancer configurations are updated to route traffic to the recovery region.
5. The VP of Engineering authorizes the switch to production traffic.

A full disaster recovery drill is conducted annually in addition to the quarterly backup restoration tests.

## 6. Responsibilities

| Role | Responsibility |
|---|---|
| IT Operations | Configure, monitor, and test backups; execute recovery procedures |
| Database Administrators | Validate database-specific backup integrity; assist with point-in-time recovery |
| System Owners | Classify their systems into the correct tier; validate recovery priorities |
| Information Security | Audit backup encryption and access controls |

## 7. Policy Governance

This policy is reviewed annually by IT Operations and the Information Security team. Questions should be directed to it-ops@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.2 — Last revised June 1, 2023*
title: Backup and Recovery Policy

# Bereavement Leave Policy

**Effective Date:** April 1, 2021
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees (full-time and part-time)

## 1. Paid Bereavement Leave

Employees are granted paid bereavement leave following the death of a family member:

| Relationship | Paid Days |
|---|---|
| Immediate family (spouse, domestic partner, child, parent, sibling) | Up to 5 days |
| Extended family (grandparent, grandchild, in-law, aunt, uncle, niece, nephew) | Up to 3 days |

Leave days need not be consecutive but must be taken within 30 calendar days of the date of death or memorial service.

## 2. Additional Unpaid Leave

Employees who need time beyond the paid allotment may request additional unpaid bereavement leave of up to 5 business days. Requests are submitted to the employee's manager and Human Resources. Approval is at manager discretion based on individual circumstances.

Employees may also use accrued vacation or sick leave to extend their paid absence.

## 3. Notification

Employees should notify their manager as soon as practicable. Human Resources may request documentation (e.g., obituary, funeral program, or death certificate) for absences exceeding 3 days.

## 4. Travel Considerations

Employees who need to travel a significant distance (more than 500 miles one way) for funeral arrangements or services may request 1 additional paid travel day per trip, subject to manager approval.

## 5. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com.
title: Bereavement Leave Policy

text:
Acme Corp — Billing FAQ
  



Billing FAQ

Last Updated: January 15, 2025



  Billing Cycles


  When does my billing cycle start?

  
    Your billing cycle starts on the date you subscribe. Monthly plans are billed on the same calendar date each month. Annual plans are billed in full at the start of the contract and renew on the anniversary of the original subscription date.

  


  Can I change my billing cycle from monthly to annual?

  
    Yes. Switch to annual billing at any time under Billing > Subscription > Change Plan. The annual rate takes effect immediately, and you receive a prorated credit for the unused portion of your current monthly billing period. Annual billing saves approximately 10% compared to monthly billing.

  





  Invoicing


  How do I access my invoices?

  
    Invoices are available in your account under Billing > Invoices. Each invoice is generated automatically after a payment is processed and includes a breakdown of charges by line item (base subscription, add-ons, usage overages, prorated adjustments).

  


  Can I have invoices sent to my finance team automatically?

  
    Yes. Under Billing > Settings > Invoice Recipients, add up to 5 email addresses. Invoices are sent to all listed recipients within 24 hours of generation.

  


  Do you support purchase orders?

  
    Purchase orders are supported for Enterprise customers. Provide your PO number during contract negotiation and it will appear on all invoices for that contract. For self-serve and Pro plans, PO numbers are not supported.

  





  Payment Methods


  What payment methods are accepted?

  
    
      MethodAvailable On

      Visa, Mastercard, American ExpressAll plans

      ACH bank transferAll plans

      Wire transferEnterprise only

      Purchase order / net termsEnterprise only

    
    All credit card payments are processed by our PCI-compliant payment processor. Acme Corp does not store credit card numbers.

  


  How do I update my payment method?

  
    Go to Billing > Payment Method > Update. The new payment method is used for all future charges. Outstanding invoices already issued are charged to the method on file at the time of issue.

  





  Proration


  What happens when I add seats mid-cycle?

  
    When you add seats, the charge is prorated for the remaining days in the current billing period. For example, if you add 5 seats on day 15 of a 30-day monthly cycle on the Pro plan ($59/seat/month), the prorated charge is: 5 seats × $59 × (15/30) = $147.50. The full per-seat rate applies from the next billing cycle.

  


  What happens when I remove seats?

  
    Removed seats take effect at the end of the current billing period. No prorated credit is issued for the remaining days. The reduced seat count is reflected in the next invoice.

  


  What happens when I upgrade my plan?

  
    Upgrades are effective immediately. You are charged the prorated difference between your current plan and the new plan for the remainder of the billing period. The new plan rate applies in full from the next cycle.

  


  What happens when I downgrade my plan?

  
    Downgrades take effect at the start of the next billing cycle. You retain access to higher-tier features until the current period ends. No refund or credit is issued for the current period.

  





  Usage Overages


  What counts as a usage overage?

  
    Overages occur when your account exceeds the included limits for your plan in a billing period:

    
      ResourceStarter LimitPro LimitEnterprise

      API requests per day10,000100,000Custom

      Data export per month1 GB10 GBUnlimited

      Storage5 GB50 GBCustom

    
  


  How are overages billed?

  
    Overages are calculated at the end of each billing period and added to the next invoice:

    
      ResourceOverage Rate

      API requests$0.001 per request over limit

      Data export$0.10 per GB over limit

      Storage$0.05 per GB per month over limit

    
    To avoid unexpected charges, set up usage alerts under Billing > Usage > Alerts. You can receive notifications at 75%, 90%, and 100% of your plan limits.

  


  Does the recent pricing change affect overages?

  
    The Pro tier pricing change (from $49 to $59/seat/month, effective November 1, 2024) applies to the base subscription only. Overage rates are unchanged. Plan limits (API requests, export, storage) are also unchanged. See the pricing update announcement for full details.

  





  Contact

  For billing questions, contact billing-support@acmecorp.com.
title: Billing FAQ

# Bring Your Own Device (BYOD) Policy

**Effective Date:** August 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees and contractors who wish to use personal devices for work

## 1. Eligibility

Employees and contractors may use personal devices (smartphones, tablets, laptops) to access Acme Corp systems, provided the devices are enrolled in the company's Mobile Device Management (MDM) program.

Enrollment is voluntary. Employees who do not wish to enroll personal devices must use company-issued equipment exclusively.

## 2. MDM Enrollment

Before accessing any company system from a personal device, users must:

1. Install the MDM agent (Jamf for macOS/iOS, Microsoft Intune for Windows/Android) from the IT self-service portal.
2. Complete the enrollment process, which configures security policies on the device.
3. Pass the device posture check: OS version must be within two major versions of current, disk encryption must be enabled, and a screen lock must be active.

Enrollment typically takes 10–15 minutes. IT can assist during office hours or via the #it-help Slack channel.

## 3. Security Requirements for Enrolled Devices

Enrolled personal devices must maintain:

- Full disk encryption (FileVault, BitLocker, or Android encryption).
- A screen lock with a PIN, password, or biometric authentication, activating after no more than 5 minutes of inactivity.
- The MDM agent installed and active at all times.
- Operating system updates applied within 14 days of release.
- No jailbroken or rooted operating systems.

Devices that fall out of compliance are automatically blocked from accessing company systems until the issue is resolved.

## 4. Data Handling

- Company data accessed on personal devices is subject to the same classification and handling rules as data on company-managed devices (see the Data Classification Policy).
- Users must not store Restricted data locally on personal devices. Restricted data should be accessed only through company-managed applications and cloud services.
- Confidential data may be cached locally by approved applications (e.g., email, Slack) but must be encrypted by the application.

## 5. Data Wiping on Separation

When an employee or contractor separates from Acme Corp:

- The MDM profile and all company-managed applications are remotely removed from the personal device.
- Company data within managed applications (email accounts, managed app data, VPN configuration) is wiped.
- Personal data, photos, and non-managed applications are not affected.

Users are notified before the remote wipe is initiated. If the device is lost or stolen, IT may perform a selective wipe without advance notice.

## 6. Privacy

Acme Corp's MDM program has visibility into:

- Device model, OS version, and encryption status.
- Installed managed applications.
- Compliance status (posture checks).

The MDM program does **not** have visibility into:

- Personal email, messages, or photos.
- Browsing history.
- Non-managed applications.
- Device location (location tracking is disabled in the MDM configuration).

## 7. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by IT Operations. Questions should be directed to it-support@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.1 — Last revised August 1, 2023*
title: BYOD Policy
