legal review
3.3 Data Retention
Data retention follows the Data Retention Policy. In general, business records are retained for
7 years. EU customer personal data is retained for 3 years. Refer to the Data Retention Policy
for full requirements.
4. Access Controls and Authentication
4.1 Access Control Principles
Acme Corp enforces access controls based on the principle of least privilege. Users are granted
the minimum level of access necessary to perform their job functions. Access controls are
reviewed quarterly by system owners.
Access provisioning follows this process:
1. Employee or manager submits an access request through the IT portal.
2. The system owner reviews and approves or denies the request within 2 business days.
3. IT provisions the approved access.
4. Access is logged in the identity management system.
Role changes (promotion, transfer, termination) trigger an automatic access review. IT revokes
access that is no longer required within 1 business day of the role change notification.
4.2 Authentication Standards
4.2.1 Password Requirements
All Acme Corp systems that support password-based au-
thentication must enforce the following requirements:
3


• Minimum length of 12 characters.
• Must include at least one uppercase letter, one lowercase letter, one number, and
one special character (e.g., !, @, #, $, %, ^, &, *).
• Passwords must be rotated every 90 days.
• Users may not reuse any of their last 10 passwords.
• Passwords must not contain the user’s name, username, or common dictionary words.
• Temporary passwords issued by IT must be changed on first login.
• Passwords must not be shared, written down, or stored in unencrypted form.
Systems that do not support the full password complexity requirements listed above must
implement compensating controls (e.g., longer minimum length, hardware token requirement)
approved by the InfoSec team.
4.2.2 Multi-Factor Authentication (MFA)
Multi-factor authentication is required for:
• All remote access to company systems (VPN, cloud applications).
• All access to Confidential and Restricted data.
• All administrative and privileged accounts.
• All access to production infrastructure.
Supported MFA methods include hardware security keys (preferred), authenticator apps (TOTP),
and push notifications. SMS-based MFA is not permitted due to known vulnerabilities.
4.2.3 Service Accounts and API Keys
Service accounts and API keys must:
• Be tied to a specific system or application, not to an individual.
• Have a designated human owner responsible for rotation and decommissioning.
• Be stored in the company’s secrets management system (HashiCorp Vault). Hardcoded
credentials in source code are prohibited.
• Be rotated at least every 180 days, or immediately if compromise is suspected.
4.2.4 Session Management
• Idle sessions must time out after 15 minutes of inactivity for systems handling Confidential
or Restricted data, and 30 minutes for Internal systems.
• Concurrent sessions are limited to 3 per user for web applications.
• Session tokens must be invalidated on logout and on password change.
5. Network Security
5.1 Network Segmentation
The corporate network is segmented into the following zones:
• Corporate zone: Employee workstations, printers, conference room equipment.
• Production zone: Application servers, databases, load balancers. Accessible only from
the deployment pipeline and authorized operations personnel.
• DMZ: Public-facing web servers, API gateways, CDN origins.
• Management zone: Monitoring systems, logging infrastructure, secrets management.
Accessible only from the management VPN.
Traffic between zones is controlled by firewall rules reviewed quarterly by the InfoSec team.
4


5.2 VPN Requirements
All remote access to internal systems must use the company VPN. The VPN enforces:
• MFA at connection time.
• Device posture checks (OS version, disk encryption, endpoint agent).
• Split tunneling is disabled for connections to the production and management zones.
5.3 Wireless Security
Corporate wireless networks use WPA3-Enterprise with certificate-based authentication. Guest
wireless is isolated from the corporate network and provides internet access only.
6. Encryption Standards
6.1 Data at Rest
All Confidential and Restricted data must be encrypted at rest using AES-256 or an equivalent
algorithm. This includes:
• Database storage (transparent data encryption or application-level encryption).
• File storage (encrypted volumes or object-level encryption).
• Laptop hard drives (full disk encryption required per the Laptop Security Requirements).
• Backup media.
6.2 Data in Transit
All data in transit must be encrypted using TLS 1.3 (preferred) or TLS 1.2 with approved cipher
suites. TLS 1.0 and 1.1 are prohibited. Internal service-to-service communication within the
production zone must use mutual TLS (mTLS).
6.3 Key Management
Encryption keys are managed through HashiCorp Vault. Key management practices include:
• Separation of duties between key administrators and key users.
• Automatic key rotation every 365 days for data encryption keys.
• Immediate rotation if key compromise is suspected.
• Key escrow for disaster recovery purposes, stored in a geographically separate secure
facility.
7. Endpoint Security
7.1 Company-Managed Devices
All company-issued devices must have:
• Full disk encryption enabled.
• The company endpoint detection and response (EDR) agent installed and active.
• Automatic OS and application updates enabled.
• Screen lock activating after 5 minutes of inactivity.
5


7.2 Personal Devices (BYOD)
Personal devices used to access company systems must be enrolled in the Mobile Device Man-
agement (MDM) program. Enrolled devices must meet the same encryption, EDR, and update
requirements as company-managed devices. Refer to the BYOD Policy.
7.3 Removable Media
The use of removable media (USB drives, external hard drives) is prohibited on company-
managed devices unless explicitly approved by the InfoSec team for a specific business pur-
pose. Approved removable media must be encrypted.
8. Vulnerability Management and Patching
8.1 Vulnerability Scanning
The InfoSec team conducts automated vulnerability scans of all production systems weekly
and all corporate systems monthly. Critical and high-severity findings are triaged within 24
hours of detection.
8.2 Patch Cadence
Security patches must be applied within the following timelines:
Severity
Patch Deadline
Critical (CVSS 9.0+)
7 days
High (CVSS 7.0–8.9)
30 days
Medium (CVSS 4.0–6.9)
Monthly patch window
Low (CVSS < 4.0)
Next quarterly patch cycle
Exceptions to the patch timeline require a documented risk acceptance signed by the system
owner and the VP of Engineering.
8.3 Penetration Testing
External penetration tests are conducted annually by an independent third party.
Internal
penetration tests are conducted semi-annually by the InfoSec team. Findings are tracked to
remediation and verified by re-test.
9. Logging and Auditing
9.1 Audit Logging Requirements
All systems handling Confidential or Restricted data must maintain audit logs that capture:
• Authentication events (login, logout, failed attempts).
• Authorization events (access granted, access denied).
• Data access events (read, write, delete operations on sensitive records).
• Administrative actions (configuration changes, user provisioning).
• System events (startup, shutdown, errors).
6


Audit logs must include a timestamp (UTC), user identity, source IP, action performed, and the
resource affected.
9.2 Log Retention
Audit logs are retained for 1 year in hot storage (searchable, indexed) and 6 years in cold
storage (archived, retrievable within 72 hours). Total retention of 7 years meets regulatory
requirements for SOC 2 and HIPAA. See the Audit Log Retention document for archival proce-
dures.
9.3 Log Integrity
Audit logs must be written to append-only storage. Logs must not be modifiable or deletable
by the system or users whose activity they record. The logging infrastructure is in the man-
agement zone, isolated from the systems it monitors.
9.4 Monitoring and Alerting
The InfoSec team operates a Security Information and Event Management (SIEM) system that
aggregates logs from all production and corporate systems. Automated alerts are configured
for:
• Multiple failed login attempts (5+ within 10 minutes).
• Access to Restricted data outside business hours.
• Privilege escalation events.
• Large data exports (>1 GB from any single system).
• Configuration changes to production infrastructure.
Alerts are triaged by the on-call security engineer. Critical alerts are escalated to the InfoSec
team lead within 15 minutes.
10. Incident Response
10.1 Incident Classification
Security incidents are classified by severity:
Severity
Definition
Response Time
SEV1 — Critical
Active data breach,
ransomware, or system
compromise affecting
production
Immediate (15 min to
assemble response team)
SEV2 — High
Confirmed vulnerability
exploitation, unauthorized
access to Restricted data
1 hour
SEV3 — Medium
Suspicious activity, policy
violation, phishing success
4 hours
SEV4 — Low
Failed attack, minor policy
deviation, security question
Next business day
7


10.2 Incident Response Process
The full incident response process is documented in the Incident Response Runbook. The sum-
mary process is:
1. Detection and reporting: Any employee who suspects a security incident reports it to
security@acmecorp.com or the #security-incidents Slack channel.
2. Triage: The on-call security engineer assesses severity and assigns an incident comman-
der.
3. Containment: Immediate actions to limit the scope and impact (isolate affected systems,
revoke compromised credentials, block malicious IPs).
4. Investigation: Root cause analysis, forensic evidence preservation, scope determina-
tion.
5. Remediation: Fix the vulnerability, restore affected systems, verify the fix.
6. Communication: Notify affected parties (internal stakeholders, customers, regulators)
as required.
7. Postmortem: Blameless postmortem conducted within 5 business days. Action items
tracked to completion.
10.3 Breach Notification
In the event of a confirmed data breach involving personal data:
• The Data Protection Officer is notified within 4 hours of confirmation.
• Regulatory notifications (e.g., GDPR 72-hour notification, HIPAA notification) are coordi-
nated by the Chief Compliance Officer and Legal.
• Affected individuals are notified as required by applicable law.
11. Physical Security
11.1 Office Access
Access to Acme Corp offices is controlled by badge readers. Visitors must sign in at reception
and be escorted at all times. Tailgating (following someone through a badge-controlled door)
is prohibited; employees must badge in individually.
11.2 Server Rooms and Data Centers
Server rooms are restricted to authorized IT personnel. Access requires badge + biometric
authentication. All entry and exit is logged. Visitors to server rooms must be accompanied by
an authorized IT staff member at all times.
12. Compliance and Certification
12.1 Certifications
Acme Corp maintains the following security certifications:
• SOC 2 Type II: Audit completed March 2024. Covers the Trust Services Criteria for Secu-
rity, Availability, and Confidentiality.
• ISO 27001: Certification renewed January 2024. Covers the information security man-
agement system (ISMS).
8


• HIPAA: Compliance maintained for all systems processing protected health information
(PHI) on behalf of healthcare customers.
12.2 Audit Schedule
Audit
Frequency
Next Scheduled
SOC 2 Type II
Annual
March 2025
ISO 27001 surveillance
Annual
January 2025
Internal security audit
Semi-annual
June 2025
External penetration test
Annual
September 2025
HIPAA risk assessment
Annual
April 2025
12.3 Regulatory Alignment
This policy is aligned with the requirements of:
• NIST Cybersecurity Framework (CSF) 2.0
• CIS Controls v8
• GDPR (for EU customer data)
• HIPAA Security Rule (for PHI)
• SOX (for financial system controls)
13. Policy Governance
13.1 Review Cycle
This policy is reviewed annually by the InfoSec team and approved by the VP of Engineering
and the Chief Compliance Officer. Ad-hoc reviews are triggered by significant security incidents,
regulatory changes, or organizational restructuring.
13.2 Exceptions
Exceptions to any requirement in this policy must be documented using the Security Exception
Request form, approved by the InfoSec team lead and the VP of Engineering, and reviewed
every 90 days. Exceptions are logged in the security exception register.
13.3 Enforcement
Violations of this policy may result in disciplinary action up to and including termination. Vio-
lations that result in a data breach or regulatory non-compliance may also result in personal
liability under applicable law.
13.4 Contact
Questions about this policy should be directed to security@acmecorp.com or the InfoSec
team’s Slack channel (#infosec).

# Jury Duty Leave Policy

**Effective Date:** June 15, 2022
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees (full-time and part-time)

## 1. Paid Jury Duty Leave

Employees receive full pay for up to 10 days of jury duty per year. This applies to service on any federal, state, or local jury, including grand jury duty. Days need not be consecutive.

Jury duty beyond 10 days in a calendar year is unpaid but job-protected. Employees may use accrued vacation to cover unpaid jury duty days at their discretion.

## 2. Notification and Documentation

Employees must:

1. Notify their manager and Human Resources within 2 business days of receiving a jury duty summons.
2. Provide a copy of the summons to Human Resources before the service date.
3. Submit proof of attendance (court-issued certificate or signed statement) within 3 business days of completing service for each day or block of days served.

Failure to provide documentation may result in the absence being recorded as unexcused.

## 3. Work Expectations During Jury Service

Employees are not required to work on days they report for jury duty. On days where the employee is dismissed early (before 1:00 PM local time), they should contact their manager to discuss whether returning to work for the remainder of the day is practical. There is no obligation to return, but the company appreciates the effort when feasible.

Employees are not expected to work evenings or weekends to compensate for time spent on jury duty.

## 4. Return to Work

Employees return to their regular position and schedule on the next business day after jury duty ends. No formal reinstatement process is required. If jury service exceeded 10 consecutive business days, the manager and employee should schedule a brief check-in to review any project updates or priority changes that occurred during the absence.

## 5. Retaliation Prohibited

Acme Corp prohibits any adverse action against an employee for serving on a jury. This includes termination, demotion, reduction in hours, or any form of discipline related to jury service.

## 6. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com.
title: Jury Duty Leave Policy

text:
# Laptop Security Requirements

**Effective Date:** January 15, 2024
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp-issued laptops and workstations

## 1. Full Disk Encryption

All company-issued laptops must have full disk encryption enabled:

- **macOS:** FileVault 2. IT enables this during device provisioning. Do not disable FileVault.
- **Windows:** BitLocker with TPM. IT enables this during device provisioning. Do not disable BitLocker.
- **Linux:** LUKS encryption on the root partition. Configured during IT provisioning.

Recovery keys are escrowed in the company's device management system. If you forget your disk encryption password, contact IT for recovery.

## 2. Screen Lock

Devices must lock automatically after 5 minutes of inactivity. The screen lock must require the user's password, PIN, or biometric authentication to unlock.

Do not disable the screen lock or extend the timeout. Manually lock your device (Cmd+Ctrl+Q on macOS, Win+L on Windows) when stepping away from your desk.

## 3. Endpoint Agent

The company's endpoint detection and response (EDR) agent must be installed and active at all times. The EDR agent:

- Monitors for malware, ransomware, and suspicious process activity.
- Reports device posture to the VPN and MDM systems.
- Is required for VPN connection and access to internal systems.

If the EDR agent is stopped, quarantined, or uninstalled, the device is automatically blocked from the corporate network. Contact IT to resolve agent issues.

## 4. Software Updates

Automatic OS and application updates must be enabled. Critical security updates are pushed by IT and must be installed within 48 hours of delivery. A restart may be required; IT will notify you if a forced restart is scheduled.

## 5. Loss or Theft Reporting

If your laptop is lost or stolen:

1. Report the loss immediately to IT at it-support@acmecorp.com and by calling the IT emergency line at ext. 9911.
2. IT will remotely lock and, if necessary, remotely wipe the device.
3. File a report with local law enforcement if the device was stolen.
4. Notify your manager.

Time is critical. Reporting within 1 hour of discovery enables IT to lock the device before credentials or data may be compromised.

## 6. Prohibited Modifications

Users must not:

- Disable or circumvent any security feature (encryption, EDR, screen lock, firewall).
- Install operating systems not provisioned by IT.
- Connect the laptop to unauthorized networks without VPN protection.
- Physically open the device casing or modify hardware.

## 7. Policy Governance

This document is maintained by IT Operations. Questions should be directed to it-support@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 2.0 — Last revised January 15, 2024*
title: Laptop Security Requirements

# Life Insurance Benefit

**Effective Date:** August 1, 2021
**Applies to:** All full-time Acme Corp employees

## 1. Basic Life Insurance

Acme Corp provides basic life insurance coverage equal to 1x the employee's annual base salary, at no cost to the employee. Coverage is effective on the first day of employment and remains in effect for the duration of employment.

The benefit maximum under the basic plan is $500,000. Employees whose base salary exceeds $500,000 are capped at that amount for basic coverage.

Basic life insurance includes accidental death and dismemberment (AD&D) coverage at the same level.

## 2. Supplemental Life Insurance

Employees may purchase additional voluntary life insurance through payroll deduction:

| Coverage Level | Evidence of Insurability Required |
|---|---|
| Up to 2x salary | No (guaranteed issue at initial enrollment) |
| 3x–5x salary | Yes (medical questionnaire required) |

Guaranteed issue is available only during the employee's initial enrollment window (within 30 days of hire) or during annual open enrollment for employees who have not previously enrolled. Employees requesting coverage above 2x salary at any time must complete the evidence of insurability process.

The supplemental benefit maximum is 5x annual base salary, capped at $1,500,000.

## 3. Spousal and Dependent Coverage

Employees may also purchase life insurance for:

- **Spouse/domestic partner:** Flat amounts of $25,000, $50,000, or $100,000. Evidence of insurability is required for amounts above $25,000.
- **Dependent children (age 14 days to 26 years):** $10,000 flat benefit. No evidence of insurability required.

## 4. Beneficiary Designation

Employees must designate a primary and contingent beneficiary through the benefits portal. Beneficiary designations may be updated at any time — there is no requirement to wait for open enrollment.

If no beneficiary designation is on file at the time of death, the benefit is paid in the following order: surviving spouse, surviving children in equal shares, the employee's estate.

Employees are encouraged to review their beneficiary designations annually and after major life events (marriage, divorce, birth of a child).

## 5. Portability

Employees who separate from Acme Corp may convert their basic and supplemental life insurance to an individual policy within 31 days of their last day of employment. Conversion rates are set by the insurer and are typically higher than the group rate. Details are provided in the separation packet.

## 6. Policy Governance

Life insurance benefits are administered by Human Resources in partnership with MetLife as the group carrier. Questions should be directed to benefits@acmecorp.com or ext. 4200.
title: Life Insurance Benefit

# Mandatory Training Catalog

**Effective Date:** January 10, 2025
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp employees unless otherwise noted

## Overview

The following trainings are required for all employees or for specific roles as indicated. Training completion is tracked in the learning management system (LMS). Non-completion by the stated deadline may result in suspension of system access until the training is finished.

## Required for All Employees

## New Hire Training

All new hires must complete the following within their first 30 days:

- Security Awareness
- Harassment Prevention
- Data Privacy and Handling
- Code of Conduct Acknowledgment

Department-specific trainings must be completed within 60 days of the hire date.

## Non-Compliance

Employees who have not completed mandatory training by the stated deadline are subject to the following escalation:

1. **7 days past deadline:** Automated reminder from the LMS.
2. **14 days past deadline:** Manager notified; written warning issued.
3. **30 days past deadline:** System access suspended until training is completed.

Departments with a training completion rate below 90% at the end of a quarter are flagged in the quarterly compliance report and are required to submit a remediation plan to Human Resources within 15 business days.

## Policy Governance

Mandatory training requirements are maintained by Human Resources in collaboration with the Compliance, IT, and Legal teams. Questions should be directed to training@acmecorp.com.

MFA Enrollment Guide
  



MFA Enrollment Guide


Last Updated: April 15, 2024
Applies to: All Acme Corp employees and contractors



  1. Why MFA Is Required

  Multi-factor authentication (MFA) is required for all Acme Corp accounts. MFA protects your account by requiring a second verification method in addition to your password. It is mandatory for VPN access, cloud applications, production systems, and any system handling Confidential or Restricted data.





  2. Supported Factors

  
    MethodRecommendedNotes

    Hardware security key (YubiKey, Titan)Yes — preferredMost secure; phishing-resistant. Acme Corp provides one YubiKey per employee on request.

    Authenticator app (Google Authenticator, Authy, 1Password)YesTOTP-based; works offline. Use this if you do not have a hardware key.

    Push notification (Okta Verify)AcceptableConvenient but susceptible to push fatigue attacks. Do not approve prompts you did not initiate.

    SMSNot permittedSMS is not an approved MFA method due to SIM-swapping and interception vulnerabilities.

  




  3. Enrollment Steps

  
    Navigate to sso.acmecorp.com/settings/mfa and sign in with your Acme Corp credentials.

    Click Add Authentication Method.

    Select your preferred method:
      
        Hardware key: Insert your security key and follow the browser prompt to register it.

        Authenticator app: Scan the QR code displayed on screen with your authenticator app. Enter the 6-digit code to confirm.

        Push notification: Install Okta Verify on your phone, scan the QR code, and approve the test push.

      
    

    Enroll at least two methods (one primary, one backup). This ensures you can still log in if your primary method is unavailable.

    Generate and save your recovery codes (see Section 4).

  




  4. Recovery Codes

  During enrollment, you will be given 10 one-time recovery codes. Each code can be used once to log in if all your MFA methods are unavailable (e.g., lost phone and security key).

  
    Important: Store your recovery codes in a secure location — a password manager or a sealed envelope in a physically secure place. Do not store them in a plaintext file on your computer or in an unencrypted note on your phone.
  

  If you exhaust all recovery codes or lose access to them, contact IT at it-support@acmecorp.com for an identity-verified account recovery. This process requires in-person identity verification or a video call with a government-issued ID.





  5. Troubleshooting

  
    IssueResolution

    Authenticator code not acceptedEnsure your phone's clock is synced automatically (Settings > Date & Time > Automatic). TOTP codes are time-sensitive.

    Hardware key not recognizedTry a different USB port. Ensure your browser supports WebAuthn (Chrome, Firefox, Edge, Safari all do).

    Lost phone with authenticator appUse your backup MFA method or a recovery code. Then re-enroll a new device.

    Lost security keyUse your backup method to log in, then remove the lost key from your account and enroll a replacement.

    All methods unavailableContact IT for identity-verified account recovery.

  




  6. Support

  For MFA issues, contact IT at it-support@acmecorp.com or Slack #it-help.
title: MFA Enrollment Guide

ML Platform Architecture
ML Platform Architecture
Document Owner: Sarah Chen, VP of Engineering Last Updated: January 15, 2025 Classi-
fication: Confidential
1. Overview
The ML Platform is Acme Corp’s internal infrastructure for building, training, deploying, and
serving machine learning models.
It powers customer-facing features (semantic search,
anomaly detection, natural language query) and internal tools (support ticket routing, lead
scoring, content classification).
The platform was funded by the Q3 2024 budget reallocation of $2.1M, approved by VP of
Engineering Sarah Chen on August 15, 2024. Development began in Q3 2024 with a team of
10 engineers (6 original + 4 hired under the reallocation). Semantic search reached general
availability in July 2025.
1.1 Design Principles
• Separation of training and serving.
Training workloads run on GPU-optimized in-
stances; serving runs on CPU-optimized instances with GPU available for latency-sensitive
inference.
• Feature reuse. Features computed for one model are stored in the feature store and
reusable by other models without re-computation.
• Reproducibility. Every model training run is logged with its dataset, hyperparameters,
code version, and metrics. Any historical model can be reproduced from the artifact store.
• Incremental adoption.
Teams can use individual components (feature store, model
registry, serving) independently. Full pipeline adoption is encouraged but not required.
2. Architecture Components

￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿￿
2.1 Data Ingestion Pipeline
Responsible for extracting data from source systems, transforming it into feature-ready format,
and loading it into the feature store.
• Sources: PostgreSQL production database (read replicas), event stream (Kafka), third-
party APIs, customer-uploaded documents.
• Processing: Apache Spark for batch processing; Kafka Streams for real-time feature
computation.
• Scheduling: Airflow DAGs for batch jobs, running on a 6-hour cadence for most features.
Real-time features are processed with sub-second latency through Kafka Streams.
• Data quality: Schema validation, null checks, and distribution drift detection at ingestion
time. Failed quality checks halt the pipeline and alert the data engineering on-call.
2.2 Feature Store
The feature store manages feature computation, storage, and serving for both batch and real-
time inference.
• Offline store: Parquet files on S3, partitioned by date, for training dataset construction.
Features are versioned and immutable.
• Online store: Redis cluster for low-latency feature serving at inference time. p99 read
latency: 2ms.
• Feature registry: Central catalog of all features with metadata (owner, description, data
type, freshness SLA, source pipeline). Engineers search the registry before creating new
features to avoid duplication.
• Freshness SLAs: Batch features are updated every 6 hours.
Real-time features are
updated within 1 second of the source event.
2.3 Training Pipeline
• Frameworks: PyTorch (primary for deep learning), scikit-learn (for classical ML models),
sentence-transformers (for embedding model fine-tuning).
• Compute: Training runs on GPU-optimized instances (NVIDIA A100). Jobs are submitted
through the internal job scheduler, which manages GPU allocation and queuing.
• Experiment tracking: Every training run is logged in MLflow with hyperparameters,
training/validation metrics, dataset version, code commit hash, and model artifacts. Ex-
periments are organized by project and linked to the model registry.
• Dataset management: Training datasets are constructed from the feature store’s offline
store. Dataset versions are tracked; training runs reference a specific dataset version for
reproducibility.
2.4 Model Registry
The model registry is the single source of truth for all production and staging models.
• Stages: development →staging →production →archived.
2


• Metadata: Each registered model includes: model type, training dataset version, valida-
tion metrics, author, approval status, deployment target.
• Promotion: Moving a model from staging to production requires sign-off from the model
owner and one ML engineer peer. Automated checks verify that the new model’s valida-
tion metrics meet or exceed the current production model’s metrics.
• Rollback: If a production model degrades, the serving layer can revert to the previous
model version within 60 seconds.
2.5 Model Serving
• Serving infrastructure: Models are deployed as containerized services behind the in-
ternal API gateway. Each model endpoint handles request batching, input validation, and
response formatting.
• Latency targets: p50 < 20ms, p99 < 100ms for embedding generation; p50 < 50ms,
p99 < 200ms for classification and scoring models.
• Scaling: Serving instances auto-scale based on request queue depth. Minimum 2 repli-
cas per model for availability.
• A/B testing: The serving layer supports traffic splitting for comparing model versions in
production. Split ratios are configured in the deployment manifest.
2.6 Monitoring and Feedback
• Model performance monitoring: Tracks prediction accuracy, latency, throughput, and
error rates in Datadog. Alerts fire when metrics degrade beyond defined thresholds.
• Data drift detection: Statistical tests (Kolmogorov-Smirnov, Population Stability Index)
compare the distribution of incoming features against the training distribution. Drift alerts
trigger a model retraining evaluation.
• Feedback loops: For models where ground truth becomes available (e.g., anomaly de-
tection confirmed/dismissed by user, search result clicked/skipped), feedback is collected
and used to create retraining datasets.
3. PostgreSQL HNSW Index Parameters
The semantic search feature uses PostgreSQL with the pgvector extension for vector similarity
search. Vectors are indexed using the Hierarchical Navigable Small World (HNSW) algorithm.
The HNSW index parameters significantly affect the tradeoff between search accuracy (recall),
query latency, and index build time.
3.1 Key Parameters
3

Description
Default

ef_search

query latency.
40

3.2 Tuning Guidance
The production values above were determined through systematic benchmarking against
the Acme Corp dataset (approximately 850,000 vectors, 384 dimensions, generated by the
all-MiniLM-L6-v2 embedding model).
ef_construction: - Values below 100 produced recall@10 below 0.95, which is unacceptable
for customer-facing search. - Values above 300 increased build time by 3x with marginal recall
improvement (<0.5%). - The chosen value of 200 achieves recall@10 of 0.987 with a build
time of 45 minutes for the full index.
M: - M=16 (default) achieved recall@10 of 0.96 at ef_search=128. - M=32 achieved recall@10
of 0.987 at the same ef_search, at the cost of 2x memory usage. - M=48 and M=64 showed di-
minishing returns (<0.5% recall improvement) with significant memory increase. - The chosen
value of 32 balances recall and memory for our dataset size.
ef_search: - ef_search directly controls query latency. At M=32: - ef_search=64: p99 latency
8ms, recall@10 0.970 - ef_search=128: p99 latency 15ms, recall@10 0.987 - ef_search=256:
p99 latency 28ms, recall@10 0.994 - The chosen value of 128 meets the p99 < 50ms latency
target with headroom.
3.3 Vector Indexing Performance with Filtered Metadata
When combining HNSW vector search with metadata filters (e.g., filtering by document type,
date range, or category), performance depends on the filter selectivity:
4

Strategy: For low and medium selectivity, post-filtering (retrieve more candidates, then filter)
is efficient. For high selectivity (< 5% of vectors match), pre-filtering (filter first, then search the
reduced set) avoids the HNSW graph traversal degradation. The query planner automatically
selects the strategy based on estimated filter cardinality.
The threshold between post-filter and pre-filter strategies is configurable. The current produc-
tion threshold is set at 5% selectivity, determined through the benchmarks above.
3.4 Reindexing
Reindexing is required when:
• The embedding model is changed or updated (all vectors must be regenerated).
• HNSW parameters are changed (the index must be rebuilt).
• The dataset grows significantly (>2x since last build), which may warrant parameter re-
evaluation.
Reindexing runs in the background on a replica. The new index is swapped in atomically with
zero downtime using PostgreSQL’s REINDEX CONCURRENTLY equivalent (drop old index, rename
new index, within a transaction).

New Hire Benefits Guide
New Hire Benefits Guide
Welcome to Acme Corp. This guide walks you through the benefits available to you and the
enrollment decisions you need to make in your first 30 days.
Enrollment Window
You have 30 days from your start date to make your benefit elections. If you do not make
selections within 30 days, you will be automatically enrolled in the Basic health plan with
employee-only coverage and no supplemental benefits.
All elections are made through the benefits portal at benefits.acmecorp.com.

If you choose Basic or Standard and want dental or vision coverage, you must enroll in the
standalone dental plan ($28/month) and/or vision plan ($12/month) separately.
Coverage begins on your first day of employment. For full plan details, see the Health Plans
Comparison document.
401(k) Retirement Plan
You are automatically enrolled in the 401(k) plan at a 3% contribution rate unless you opt out
or select a different rate. Acme Corp matches 50% of your contributions up to 6% of salary
(maximum employer contribution: 3% of salary). Employer matching contributions vest over
4 years. See the 401(k) Matching Policy for details.
Health Savings Account (HSA)
If you enroll in the Basic or Standard plan, you are eligible to open an HSA. Acme Corp con-
tributes $500 (individual) or $1,000 (family) annually to your HSA. See the HSA/FSA Policy for
contribution limits and details.
1


Life Insurance
You receive basic life insurance at 1x your annual salary at no cost. You may purchase sup-
plemental coverage up to 5x salary — guaranteed issue up to 2x salary during your initial
enrollment window. See the Life Insurance Benefit document.
Equity (RSUs)
If your offer includes an RSU grant, vesting follows a 4-year schedule with a 25% cliff at year
1 and monthly vesting thereafter. See the RSU Vesting Schedule for details.
Employee Referral Program
After completing your 90-day probationary period, you are eligible for the Employee Referral
Bonus Program ($2,000 per successful referral). See the Employee Referral Bonus Program
document for requirements.
Wellness Program
You have access to gym reimbursement ($50/month), an annual health screening, and the
Employee Assistance Program (EAP) for confidential counseling. See the Wellness Program
Overview.
Remote Work
If your role is eligible for remote or hybrid work, your manager will discuss the arrangement
during your first week. Approved remote employees receive a $500 home office stipend. See
the Remote Work Policy.
Time Off
Your vacation accrual begins on day one: 15 days (120 hours) in your first year. You may not
use accrued vacation during the first 60 days except in emergencies. For sick leave, jury duty,
parental leave, and other leave types, see the relevant leave policies listed in the Employee
Handbook.
Open Enrollment
Your initial benefit elections remain in effect through December 31. You can make changes
during the annual open enrollment period, which closes November 15 each year, or within 30
days of a qualifying life event.
Questions?
• Benefits portal: benefits.acmecorp.com
• Benefits team: benefits@acmecorp.com or ext. 4200
• HR office hours: Monday–Friday, 10 AM–12 PM
2

Acme Corp — Onboarding Quickstart
  



Onboarding Quickstart

Estimated time: 10 minutes
Last Updated: February 1, 2025


This guide walks new users through setup from account creation to your first search. Follow the steps in order.



  Step 1 — Sign In

  ~1 minute

  Open app.acmecorp.com in your browser and sign in with your company email. If your organization uses SSO (Okta, Azure AD, Google Workspace), click Sign in with SSO and enter your company domain. First-time users will be prompted to set up multi-factor authentication before proceeding.





  Step 2 — Set Up Your Profile

  ~1 minute

  Click your avatar in the top right and select Profile. Add your name, job title, and department. This information is visible to your team and helps others find the right person for collaboration.





  Step 3 — Join Your Team Workspace

  ~1 minute

  Your administrator may have already assigned you to a workspace. Check the left sidebar for your team's workspace. If you do not see one, ask your administrator to add you, or create a new workspace under Settings > Workspaces > Create.





  Step 4 — Create Your First Record

  ~2 minutes

  Click the + New Record button in the top bar. Give it a name, add some content, and optionally tag it with a category. Click Save. Your record is now searchable by everyone in your workspace.





  Step 5 — Try a Search

  ~2 minutes

  Click the search bar (or press /) and type a question or keyword. If your plan supports hybrid search (Pro and above), the system combines keyword matching with semantic understanding to find the most relevant results. Try searching for the record you just created.





  Step 6 — Connect an Integration

  ~3 minutes

  Go to Settings > Integrations to see available connections. Popular starting points:

  
    Slack: Get notifications in your team channel when records are created or updated.

    Zapier: Automate workflows between Acme Corp and 5,000+ other apps.

    Email forwarding: Send emails to your workspace's unique ingest address to create records automatically.

  
  See the Integrations Catalog for the full list.





  What's Next?

  
    Explore the API documentation to build custom integrations.

    Browse the pre-built dashboard templates under Analytics > Templates.

    Review the FAQ for answers to common questions about billing, plans, and account management.

  
  Need help? Contact support@acmecorp.com or use the in-app chat (Pro and above, business hours).
title: Onboarding Quickstart

text:
# On-Call Rotation Policy

**Effective Date:** July 1, 2024
**Owner:** Engineering Platform team
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp engineers on production on-call rotations

---

## 1. Rotation Structure

On-call rotations run weekly, from Monday 9:00 AM PT to the following Monday 9:00 AM PT. Two engineers are on call at all times:

- **Primary:** First responder for all production alerts.
- **Secondary:** Backup if the primary does not acknowledge an alert within 5 minutes. Also available for parallel investigation during SEV1/SEV2 incidents.

Rotations are managed in PagerDuty. Each team with a production service maintains its own rotation. Cross-team escalation follows the Incident Response Runbook.

## 2. Eligibility

All engineers L2 and above who own or regularly contribute to a production service are eligible for on-call duty. L1 (junior) engineers may shadow an on-call rotation for training but are not assigned as primary or secondary.

New engineers must complete on-call onboarding before their first rotation:

- Read the Incident Response Runbook and the Deployment Runbook.
- Shadow one full rotation with a senior engineer.
- Complete the on-call simulation exercise (a mock SEV2 scenario).

## 3. Handoff

At each rotation change, the outgoing primary conducts a 15-minute handoff meeting with the incoming primary:

- Active incidents and their current status.
- Ongoing investigations or monitoring watch items.
- Known fragile systems or upcoming risky deployments.
- Unresolved alerts that were deferred.

Handoff notes are posted to the #oncall-handoff Slack channel.

## 4. Response Expectations

| Alert Severity | Acknowledgment SLA | Action |
|---|---|---|
| SEV1 — Critical | 5 minutes | Page secondary immediately. Begin triage. Open incident channel. |
| SEV2 — High | 15 minutes | Begin triage. Assess if secondary support is needed. |
| SEV3 — Medium | 1 hour | Investigate during business hours. After-hours: acknowledge and schedule for next business day unless escalating. |
| SEV4 — Low | Next business day | Acknowledge and add to the team backlog. |

If the primary does not acknowledge within the SLA, PagerDuty automatically pages the secondary. If neither responds within 10 minutes, the engineering manager is paged.

## 5. Compensation

On-call duty is compensated as follows:

| Component | Amount |
|---|---|
| Weekly on-call stipend (primary or secondary) | $500 |
| Per-incident bonus (SEV1 response) | $200 per incident |
| Per-incident bonus (SEV2 response) | $100 per incident |
| Comp time for after-hours incidents | 1 hour of comp time per hour of active incident work outside 9 AM – 6 PM PT |

Compensation is processed through payroll on a monthly basis. Engineers log on-call incidents and hours in the engineering time-tracking tool.

## 6. Swaps and Coverage

- Swaps must be arranged at least 24 hours in advance and logged in PagerDuty.
- If an on-call engineer is sick or has an emergency, they notify their engineering manager immediately. The manager arranges coverage from the team's backup pool.
- On-call engineers should avoid scheduling vacation during their rotation. If unavoidable, arrange a swap at least 1 week in advance.

## 7. Escalation Path

If an on-call engineer needs help beyond their team's expertise:

| Escalation | Contact |
|---|---|
| Database issues | DBA on-call (separate PagerDuty rotation) |
| Infrastructure / networking | Platform on-call (separate PagerDuty rotation) |
| Security incident | InfoSec on-call (security@acmecorp.com + PagerDuty) |
| Customer-impacting incident (SEV1/SEV2) | Incident commander per the Incident Response Runbook |

## 8. Well-Being

On-call duty should not lead to burnout. Engineering managers are responsible for:

- Ensuring no engineer is on primary rotation more than once every 4 weeks (for teams with fewer than 4 members, this may require cross-team coverage arrangements).
- Reviewing on-call load quarterly. If an engineer's after-hours page count exceeds 10 per rotation consistently, the team must invest in reliability improvements.
- Encouraging engineers to take comp time promptly rather than accumulating it.

---

*Version 1.1 — Last revised July 1, 2024*
title: On-Call Rotation Policy

text:
# Open Enrollment Deadlines

**Plan Year:** 2025
**Applies to:** All benefits-eligible Acme Corp employees

## 1. Enrollment Window

The annual open enrollment period runs from November 1 through November 15. All benefit elections for the upcoming plan year must be submitted by 11:59 PM PT on November 15. Elections take effect January 1.

Employees who do not make changes during open enrollment will be automatically re-enrolled in their current plan selections. HSA and FSA contributions do not carry forward automatically and must be re-elected each year.

## 2. What You Can Change

During open enrollment, employees may:

- Enroll in, change, or waive health insurance (Basic, Standard, or Premium).
- Add or remove dependents from health, dental, or vision coverage.
- Enroll in or change supplemental life insurance coverage.
- Set or adjust HSA contributions (Basic and Standard plan enrollees only).
- Set or adjust Healthcare FSA or Dependent Care FSA contributions.
- Enroll in or cancel the standalone dental or vision plan (for employees not on the Premium plan).

## 3. Mid-Year Changes

Outside of the open enrollment window, benefit elections may only be changed within 30 days of a qualifying life event:

- Marriage or divorce.
- Birth, adoption, or foster placement of a child.
- Death of a dependent.
- Loss of coverage under a spouse's or parent's plan.
- Change in dependent eligibility (e.g., dependent aging out at 26).
- Gain or loss of Medicaid or CHIP eligibility.

Employees must submit a qualifying life event change request through the benefits portal within 30 days of the event. Supporting documentation (marriage certificate, birth certificate, court order, or letter of coverage loss) is required.

## 4. New Hire Enrollment

New employees have 30 days from their start date to make initial benefit elections. If no elections are made within 30 days, the employee is enrolled in the Basic health plan with employee-only coverage and no FSA or supplemental life insurance. 401(k) auto-enrollment at 3% proceeds independently per the 401(k) Matching Policy.

## 5. Key Dates

| Date | Event |
|---|---|
| October 1 | Benefits fair and plan information published |
| November 1 | Open enrollment opens |
| November 15 | Open enrollment closes (11:59 PM PT) |
| December | Confirmation statements distributed |
| January 1 | New elections take effect |

## 6. Resources

- Benefits portal: benefits.acmecorp.com
- Plan comparison tool: available on the benefits portal starting October 1
- Benefits team: benefits@acmecorp.com or ext. 4200
- HR office hours during open enrollment: Monday–Friday, 10 AM–12 PM and 2 PM–4 PM
