# Shipping Policy

**Effective Date:** October 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All physical product orders (hardware kits, welcome packages, branded merchandise) shipped by Acme Corp

---

## 1. Standard Shipping

Standard shipping takes 5–7 business days from the date the order is processed. Processing typically occurs within 1 business day of order confirmation. Standard shipping is provided at no cost for all domestic (U.S.) orders.

Tracking information is sent via email once the shipment is dispatched.

## 2. Expedited Shipping

| Option | Delivery Time | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| 2-Day | 2 business days from processing | $15 per shipment |
| Overnight | Next business day by 3:00 PM local time | $35 per shipment |

Expedited orders placed before 12:00 PM PT are processed the same day. Orders placed after 12:00 PM PT are processed the next business day.

Saturday and Sunday deliveries are not available for expedited shipments.

## 3. International Shipping

Acme Corp ships to the following regions:

- **Canada:** 7–12 business days. Flat rate $20.
- **European Union:** 10–15 business days. Flat rate $35.
- **United Kingdom:** 10–15 business days. Flat rate $35.
- **Australia / New Zealand:** 12–18 business days. Flat rate $45.

International shipments may be subject to customs duties, import taxes, and brokerage fees assessed by the destination country. These charges are the responsibility of the recipient and are not included in the shipping cost.

Acme Corp does not currently ship to countries subject to U.S. trade sanctions.

## 4. Order Tracking

All shipments include tracking. Tracking numbers are emailed to the recipient within 24 hours of dispatch. Tracking is available at the carrier's website (UPS for domestic, DHL for international).

## 5. Lost or Damaged Shipments

If a shipment is lost or arrives damaged:

1. Contact operations@acmecorp.com within 14 days of the expected delivery date.
2. Provide the order number and a description (and photos, if damaged).
3. Acme Corp will file a carrier claim and ship a replacement at no cost within 3 business days of the report.

## 6. Policy Governance

This policy is maintained by the Operations team. Questions should be directed to operations@acmecorp.com.
title: Shipping Policy

text:
# Short-Term Disability Policy

**Effective Date:** January 10, 2023
**Applies to:** All benefits-eligible Acme Corp employees

## 1. Benefit Summary

Employees who are unable to work due to a qualifying medical condition receive short-term disability (STD) benefits of 60% of base salary for up to 12 weeks, following a 7-day waiting period. Benefits are paid biweekly through standard payroll.

The 7-day waiting period begins on the first day the employee is unable to work. Employees may use accrued sick leave or vacation to cover the waiting period.

## 2. Eligibility

All benefits-eligible employees (full-time and part-time working 20+ hours per week) are eligible for STD from their date of hire. There is no tenure requirement.

## 3. Qualifying Conditions

STD covers the employee's own medical condition that prevents them from performing the essential functions of their job. Examples include:

- Surgery and post-surgical recovery.
- Serious illness or injury.
- Pregnancy-related medical complications (pre- and post-delivery).
- Mental health conditions requiring inpatient treatment or intensive outpatient programs.

STD does not cover absences related to caring for a family member. Employees needing leave to care for others should refer to the FMLA Leave Procedure.

## 4. Claims Process

1. **Notify HR** of the expected absence as soon as practicable.
2. **Submit a claim form** and medical documentation from the treating provider within 10 calendar days of the first day of disability. The documentation must include diagnosis, treatment plan, expected duration, and a statement that the employee is unable to perform their job duties.
3. **HR and the STD administrator** review the claim and issue a determination within 5 business days.
4. If approved, benefits begin after the 7-day waiting period and continue for the duration certified by the provider, up to the 12-week maximum.

Employees must submit updated medical documentation every 30 days for ongoing claims.

## 5. Coordination with Other Benefits

STD benefits coordinate with other Acme Corp leave programs:

- **FMLA:** STD pay often runs concurrently with FMLA leave. FMLA provides unpaid job protection; STD provides income replacement. An employee with a qualifying condition typically receives both: job protection from FMLA and 60% pay from STD.
- **Parental leave:** Birthing parents with pregnancy-related complications may receive STD benefits during the medical recovery portion of their leave, while parental leave pay (100% of salary) covers the bonding portion.
- **Vacation/sick leave:** Employees may supplement STD pay with accrued vacation or sick leave to reach up to 100% of base salary. This is optional.

## 6. How STD Differs from FMLA

| | Short-Term Disability | FMLA |
|---|---|---|
| **What it provides** | Income replacement (60% of salary) | Unpaid job protection |
| **Duration** | Up to 12 weeks after 7-day waiting period | Up to 12 weeks per 12-month period |
| **Eligibility** | All benefits-eligible employees from hire | 12 months of tenure required |
| **Covers family care** | No — employee's own condition only | Yes — employee or qualifying family member |
| **Job protection** | No independent protection | Yes — same or equivalent role guaranteed |

In practice, most employees who qualify for STD also qualify for concurrent FMLA leave, receiving both income replacement and job protection.

## 7. Return to Work

Employees must obtain a fitness-for-duty certification from their treating provider before returning. The certification must confirm the employee can perform the essential functions of their role, with or without reasonable accommodations.

If the employee requires accommodations upon return, Human Resources will coordinate with the employee and their manager through the interactive accommodation process.

## 8. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Human Resources in partnership with the company's STD administrator. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com.
title: Short-Term Disability Policy

text:
# Sick Leave Policy

**Effective Date:** January 15, 2022
**Applies to:** All full-time Acme Corp employees

## 1. Accrual

Full-time employees accrue sick leave at a rate of 1 day per month (8 hours), up to a maximum balance of 15 days (120 hours). Sick leave does not accrue beyond the 15-day cap. Unused sick leave carries over year to year but is not paid out upon separation.

Part-time employees accrue sick leave on a prorated basis relative to scheduled weekly hours.

## 2. Permitted Use

Sick leave may be used for:

- The employee's own illness, injury, or medical appointment.
- Care of an immediate family member (spouse, child, parent) with an illness or medical appointment.
- Mental health days, limited to 3 per calendar year without medical documentation.

Sick leave may not be used for vacation purposes. Patterns of misuse (e.g., repeated absences on Mondays or Fridays) may be flagged for review by the employee's manager and Human Resources.

## 3. Notification

Employees must notify their manager before the start of their scheduled shift, or as soon as practicable. Notification by email, phone, or the company's messaging system is acceptable. For planned medical appointments, employees should provide at least 2 business days' notice.

## 4. Documentation

A doctor's note is required for absences exceeding 3 consecutive days. The note must confirm the employee was seen by a provider and include an expected return-to-work date. Detailed diagnosis information is not required.

Human Resources may request documentation for shorter absences if a pattern of concern has been identified and documented by the employee's manager.

## 5. Coordination with Other Leave

- Sick leave may be used to cover the 7-day waiting period under the Short-Term Disability Policy.
- Sick leave runs concurrently with FMLA leave when applicable.
- Employees who exhaust sick leave may use accrued vacation for continued absences.

## 6. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com.
title: Sick Leave Policy

text:
# SLA Dashboard Guide

**Last Updated:** June 15, 2024
**Applies to:** Enterprise customers with an active Enterprise SLA

## 1. Accessing the Dashboard

The SLA dashboard is available at **app.acmecorp.com/sla** for account owners and administrators on Enterprise plans. The dashboard provides a real-time view of your account's uptime, latency, and service credit status.

## 2. Uptime Metrics

The uptime panel shows:

- **Current month uptime:** Percentage of uptime for the current calendar month, updated every 5 minutes. Calculated as: `(Total Minutes − Downtime Minutes) / Total Minutes × 100`.
- **Rolling 12-month uptime:** Average uptime across the last 12 calendar months.
- **Uptime history:** Month-by-month uptime percentage for the last 12 months, displayed as a bar chart. Months where uptime dropped below the 99.95% guarantee are highlighted in red.

Downtime is measured from the Acme Corp monitoring infrastructure. Scheduled maintenance windows (communicated at least 72 hours in advance) are excluded from the downtime calculation per the Enterprise SLA, Section 1.3.

## 3. Latency Metrics

The latency panel shows API response time for your account's production endpoints:

| Metric | Definition | SLA Target |
|---|---|---|
| p50 (median) | 50th percentile response time | < 100ms |
| p95 | 95th percentile response time | < 250ms |
| p99 | 99th percentile response time | < 500ms |

Latency is measured at the Acme Corp load balancer and does not include network transit time between your infrastructure and ours. Data is aggregated in 5-minute intervals and displayed for the last 24 hours, 7 days, and 30 days.

## 4. Service Credit Balance

If uptime in a calendar month falls below 99.95%, your account may be eligible for service credits per the Enterprise SLA, Section 2:

| Monthly Uptime | Credit |
|---|---|
| 99.90% – 99.95% | 10% of monthly fee |
| 99.00% – 99.89% | 25% of monthly fee |
| Below 99.00% | 50% of monthly fee |

The dashboard displays:

- **Earned credits:** Credits accrued from SLA breaches in the current and prior 12 months.
- **Applied credits:** Credits already deducted from invoices.
- **Available balance:** Credits eligible to be applied to the next invoice.

Credits are not applied automatically. To apply a credit, click **Request Credit** on the dashboard or email sla-credits@acmecorp.com within 30 days of the affected month.

## 5. Incident History

The incident history panel lists all incidents that affected your account in the last 12 months:

- Incident date and time.
- Duration.
- Severity (SEV1–SEV4).
- Root cause summary (linked to the public postmortem, if published).
- Whether the incident resulted in downtime counted against the SLA.

## 6. Notifications

Configure SLA notifications under **SLA Dashboard > Settings > Alerts**:

- **Uptime alert:** Receive an email when current-month uptime drops below a threshold you set (default: 99.97%).
- **Latency alert:** Receive an email when p99 latency exceeds 500ms for more than 10 consecutive minutes.
- **Credit eligibility:** Receive an email at month-end if your account has earned a new service credit.

Notifications are sent to all account administrators by default. Additional recipients can be added in the alert settings.

## 7. FAQ

**Q: Why does the dashboard show different uptime than the status page?**
A: The public status page (status.acmecorp.com) shows platform-wide uptime. The SLA dashboard shows uptime specific to the endpoints and services covered by your Enterprise SLA. Minor incidents that affect one customer's workload may not register on the platform-wide status page.

**Q: How long do I have to request a credit?**
A: Credit requests must be submitted within 30 days of the end of the affected month. Credits not requested within this window are forfeited.

**Q: Can I export SLA data for internal reporting?**
A: Yes. Click **Export** on any panel to download the data as CSV. Monthly SLA summary reports are also available as PDF under **SLA Dashboard > Reports**.

---

*For questions about the SLA dashboard or your service credit balance, contact your Customer Success Manager or email sla-credits@acmecorp.com.*
title: SLA Dashboard Guide

text:
Termination Procedures
Termination Procedures
Effective Date: March 1, 2023 Applies to: All Acme Corp employees
1. Voluntary Resignation
Employees who wish to resign should provide at least 2 weeks’ written notice to their manager and Human Resources. Resignations are submitted via the HR portal or by email to hr@acmecorp.com.
Upon receiving notice, the manager should:
Acknowledge the resignation in writing within 1 business day.
Begin transition planning, including knowledge transfer and reassignment of responsibilities.
Notify IT to schedule system access revocation for the employee’s last day.
2. Involuntary Termination
Involuntary terminations may result from:
Performance: After completion of a Performance Improvement Plan (PIP) without satisfactory improvement.
Policy violation: Serious or repeated violations of company policies, including the Code of Conduct, harassment policy, or data handling policies.
Position elimination: Business restructuring, role elimination, or reduction in force.
Probationary period: Failure to meet expectations during the 90-day probationary period.
All involuntary terminations require prior approval from Human Resources and the relevant department head. Terminations of L6 and above additionally require approval from the Chief People Officer.
3. Exit Interview
Human Resources conducts an exit interview with every departing employee, regardless of the reason for separation. The interview covers:
Reason for departure (voluntary separations).
Feedback on management, team, and work environment.
Confirmation that the employee understands their post-employment obligations (confidentiality, non-solicitation, intellectual property).
Review of benefits continuation options.
Exit interviews are confidential. Aggregated themes are reported to department heads quarterly; individual responses are not shared with the departing employee’s manager.
4. Final Paycheck
Final paychecks are processed in accordance with applicable state law:
Voluntary resignation with notice: Final paycheck issued on the next regular pay date.
Involuntary termination: Final paycheck issued on the last day of employment, or as required by state law.
Final paychecks include:
All wages earned through the last day of employment.
Payout of accrued, unused vacation at the employee’s current base rate.
Any outstanding expense reimbursements that have been approved.
Sick leave balances are not paid out upon separation.
5. COBRA Notification
Departing employees and their covered dependents are eligible for continuation of health benefits under COBRA. Human Resources sends the COBRA election notice within 14 days of the qualifying event (last day of employment).
Key COBRA details:
Coverage may continue for up to 18 months (or 36 months for certain qualifying events).
The employee pays the full premium (employee + employer share) plus a 2% administrative fee.
Election must be made within 60 days of receiving the COBRA notice.
Coverage is retroactive to the date of the qualifying event if elected within the 60-day window.
6. Return of Company Property
All company property must be returned on or before the employee’s last day, including:
Laptop and charger.
Mobile devices.
Access badges and keys.
Physical documents and files.
Any company data stored on personal devices (confirmed deleted per IT Security Policy).
Failure to return company property may result in deductions from the final paycheck where permitted by law, or collection action.
7. System Access Revocation
IT revokes the departing employee’s access to all company systems, including email, VPN, cloud applications, and code repositories, at the end of business on the employee’s last day. For involuntary terminations, access is revoked immediately upon notification.
8. Policy Governance
These procedures are administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com.

# US Office Data Retention Schedule

**Effective Date:** June 1, 2024
**Approved by:** General Counsel
**Applies to:** All data originating from or relating to Acme Corp's US operations (San Francisco headquarters, US-based remote employees, US customers on the US-East region)

---

## 1. Purpose

This schedule provides US-specific retention periods that supplement — and in some cases override — the company-wide Data Retention Policy. Where US federal or state law requires a specific retention period, this schedule reflects the applicable requirement. Where no US-specific requirement exists, the general Data Retention Policy applies.

## 2. US Retention Periods

| Data Category | US Retention Period | Basis | General Policy Period |
|---|---|---|---|
| Customer transaction records | 7 years | IRS §6001, SOX | 7 years (same) |
| Customer account data (active) | Duration of relationship | Operational | Duration of relationship (same) |
| Customer account data (post-termination) | Deleted within 90 days | Customer Deletion Workflow | 90 days (same) |
| Customer personal identification data | 7 years | No US storage limitation equivalent to GDPR | 3 years (EU) / 7 years (general) |
| Employee personnel files | Employment + 7 years | Federal and state employment law | Employment + 7 years (same) |
| Employee payroll and tax records | 7 years | IRS, state tax agencies | 7 years (same) |
| Job applications | 2 years | EEOC, OFCCP | 2 years (same) |
| Marketing consent records | Duration of consent + 3 years | CAN-SPAM, CCPA | Duration + 3 years (same) |
| Support ticket content | 5 years | Internal policy | 3 years (EU) |
| Audit logs | 1 year hot + 6 years cold | SOC 2, HIPAA | 1 year + 6 years (same) |

## 3. Key Differences from EU Retention

The most significant difference between US and EU retention is the absence of a storage limitation principle in US law equivalent to GDPR Article 5(1)(e). In practice, this means:

- **Customer personal identification data** (name, email, phone) may be retained for the full 7-year general retention period in the US. In the EU, this data must be deleted or anonymized within 3 years of the last processing activity for its original purpose.
- **Support ticket content** is retained for 5 years in the US (to support potential litigation and product improvement) versus 3 years for EU customers.
- **Employee performance reviews** are retained for 7 years in the US (see the HR Data Retention Schedule). For EU employees, performance reviews are retained for 3 years after separation per the EU-specific override.

All other data categories follow the same retention periods in both jurisdictions.

## 4. State-Specific Requirements

Several US states impose additional retention or deletion obligations:

### 4.1 California (CCPA/CPRA)

- Acme Corp honors California Consumer Privacy Act (CCPA) and California Privacy Rights Act (CPRA) rights for California residents.
- Consumers may request deletion of personal information. Acme Corp processes deletion requests within 45 days per CPRA (compared to 90 days for general customer deletion).
- Acme Corp does not sell personal information. The "Do Not Sell or Share" opt-out is available on the website but processes zero requests to date, as no sale occurs.

### 4.2 Virginia (VCDPA)

- Virginia Consumer Data Protection Act rights (access, correction, deletion, portability) are honored for Virginia residents.
- Response deadline: 45 days, with a 45-day extension if reasonably necessary.

### 4.3 Colorado (CPA)

- Colorado Privacy Act rights are honored for Colorado residents.
- Response deadline: 45 days, with a 45-day extension.

### 4.4 Other States

Acme Corp monitors the evolving US state privacy landscape. As of the effective date of this schedule, similar laws in Connecticut, Utah, Oregon, Texas, Montana, and Iowa are tracked by Legal. When a new state law takes effect, Legal updates this schedule within 90 days.

## 5. Litigation Hold Override

US litigation hold obligations supersede all retention schedules. When the General Counsel issues a litigation hold, affected records must be preserved regardless of whether they have reached the end of their retention period. Destruction of records subject to a litigation hold may constitute spoliation of evidence and expose the company to sanctions.

Employees who receive a litigation hold notice must confirm receipt and compliance within 48 hours.

## 6. Disposal

Records that have reached the end of their US retention period are deleted per the Data Retention Policy's Data Deletion Procedures (90 calendar days from expiration, verified by the Data Protection Officer). Physical records are destroyed using certified cross-cut shredding.

## 7. Policy Governance

This schedule is maintained by Legal. It is reviewed semi-annually to incorporate new state privacy laws. Questions should be directed to legal@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.1 — Last revised June 1, 2024*
title: US Office Data Retention Schedule

text:
# Vacation Policy

**Effective Date:** March 1, 2024
**Applies to:** All full-time Acme Corp employees

## 1. Accrual Schedule

Full-time employees accrue paid vacation based on continuous years of service, measured from date of hire:

| Tenure | Annual Vacation Days | Annual Hours | Per Pay Period (Biweekly) |
|---|---|---|---|
| Year 1 | 15 days | 120 hours | 4.62 hours |
| Years 2–9 | 20 days | 160 hours | 6.15 hours |
| 10+ years | 25 days | 200 hours | 7.69 hours |

Accrual begins on the employee's first day of employment. Tier increases take effect on the anniversary of the hire date.

Part-time employees accrue vacation on a prorated basis relative to their scheduled weekly hours.

## 2. Carryover

Employees may carry over a maximum of 40 hours of unused vacation into the following calendar year. Any balance exceeding 40 hours on January 1 is forfeited. Acme Corp does not pay out forfeited vacation time.

Employees are encouraged to use their full vacation allotment each year. Managers should work with their teams to ensure vacation is scheduled throughout the year rather than concentrated in Q4.

## 3. Requesting Vacation

- Requests of fewer than 5 consecutive days require manager approval at least 5 business days in advance.
- Requests of 5 or more consecutive days require manager approval at least 2 weeks in advance.
- Managers may deny or request rescheduling of vacation based on business needs, project deadlines, or team coverage requirements.

All requests are submitted through the HR portal. Approved vacation is reflected on the team calendar automatically.

## 4. Blackout Periods

Certain teams may designate blackout periods during which vacation requests are restricted. Common blackout periods include:

- **Engineering:** The two weeks surrounding a major product release, as designated by the VP of Engineering.
- **Finance:** The last 5 business days of each fiscal quarter (March, June, September, December) and the first 10 business days of January for year-end close.
- **Customer Success:** The week of annual contract renewals for assigned accounts.

Blackout periods are published at least 30 days in advance. Exceptions require written approval from the department head.

## 5. Vacation During Probationary Period

Employees in their 90-day probationary period accrue vacation from day one but may not use accrued vacation during the first 60 days of employment except in emergencies approved by Human Resources.

## 6. Separation

Upon voluntary resignation or involuntary termination, accrued and unused vacation is paid out at the employee's current base rate in the final paycheck, in accordance with applicable state law. Forfeited carryover balances (per Section 2) are not eligible for payout.

## 7. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to hr@acmecorp.com. This policy is reviewed annually and was last updated on March 1, 2024.
title: Vacation Policy

text:
# Vendor Management Policy

**Effective Date:** April 1, 2024
**Applies to:** All Acme Corp departments that engage third-party vendors, service providers, or sub-processors

## 1. Vendor Onboarding

Before engaging a new vendor, the sponsoring department must complete the vendor onboarding process:

1. **Business justification.** The sponsoring team submits a vendor request form describing the business need, estimated annual spend, and the type of data the vendor will access (if any).
2. **Security review.** The Information Security team conducts a risk assessment based on the vendor's SOC 2 report (or equivalent), data handling practices, and incident history. Vendors accessing Confidential or Restricted data require a full security questionnaire and evidence review. Target turnaround: 10 business days.
3. **Legal review.** Legal reviews the vendor's contract terms, including liability clauses, indemnification, termination provisions, and compliance with applicable regulations.
4. **Data processing agreement.** Vendors that process personal data on behalf of Acme Corp or its customers must execute a Data Processing Agreement (DPA) before any data is shared. The DPA template is maintained by Legal (see the Data Processing Agreement Template).
5. **Approval.** Vendor onboarding requires sign-off from the sponsoring department head and, for annual spend above $50,000, the CFO.

## 2. Restrictions on Third-Party Data Sharing

Acme Corp restricts the sharing of data with third-party vendors as follows:

- **Minimum necessary.** Only the minimum data necessary for the vendor to perform their contracted services may be shared. Bulk data exports to vendors are prohibited unless explicitly approved in the DPA.
- **No onward sharing.** Vendors may not share Acme Corp data with sub-processors unless the sub-processor is listed in the DPA and Acme Corp has provided written consent. Unauthorized sub-processing is grounds for immediate contract termination.
- **Restricted data.** Personally identifiable information (PII), protected health information (PHI), and financial data classified as Restricted may only be shared with vendors who have completed a full security review, executed a DPA, and (for PHI) signed a HIPAA Business Associate Agreement.
- **No use for vendor's own purposes.** Vendors may not use Acme Corp data for product development, analytics, model training, benchmarking, or any purpose other than fulfilling the contracted scope of work.
- **Encryption in transit.** All data shared with vendors must be encrypted in transit using TLS 1.3. Data shared via file transfer must use the company's secure file exchange portal; email attachments are not an approved transfer method for Confidential or Restricted data.
- **Return or destruction.** Upon contract termination, the vendor must return or certifiably destroy all Acme Corp data within 30 days. Destruction certificates are retained by Legal for 7 years.

## 3. Ongoing Monitoring

Active vendors are monitored on a recurring basis:

| Vendor Tier | Review Frequency | Review Scope |
|---|---|---|
| Critical (access to Restricted data or core infrastructure) | Annually | Full security reassessment, DPA compliance audit, sub-processor list review |
| Standard (access to Confidential data) | Every 2 years | Security questionnaire update, contract compliance check |
| Low-risk (no data access or Public data only) | Every 3 years | Contract renewal review |

The Information Security team maintains the vendor registry and tracks review due dates. Vendors that fail a review are placed on a remediation plan with a 60-day deadline; failure to remediate results in contract termination.

## 4. Vendor Offboarding

When a vendor engagement ends:

1. The sponsoring department notifies Legal and IT at least 30 days before the contract end date.
2. The vendor returns or destroys all Acme Corp data per the DPA terms.
3. IT revokes the vendor's access to all systems.
4. Legal confirms receipt of the data destruction certificate.
5. The vendor is marked as inactive in the vendor registry.

## 5. Policy Governance

This policy is administered by Legal in coordination with Information Security and Finance. Questions should be directed to legal@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 2.0 — Last revised April 1, 2024*
title: Vendor Management Policy

VPN Access Guide
  



VPN Access Guide


Last Updated: April 1, 2024
Applies to: All Acme Corp employees and authorized contractors working remotely



  1. Overview

  All remote access to Acme Corp internal systems requires the company VPN. The VPN encrypts your connection and enforces security checks (MFA, device posture) before granting access to internal resources.





  2. Supported Clients

  
    Operating SystemClientVersion

    macOSCloudflare WARP2024.1 or later

    Windows 10/11Cloudflare WARP2024.1 or later

    Linux (Ubuntu 22.04+, Fedora 38+)Cloudflare WARP CLI2024.1 or later

    iOSCloudflare 1.1.1.1 appLatest from App Store

    AndroidCloudflare 1.1.1.1 appLatest from Play Store

  
  Third-party VPN clients are not supported. Do not use personal VPN services concurrently with the Acme Corp VPN.





  3. Enrollment Steps

  
    Open your web browser and navigate to vpn.acmecorp.com/enroll.

    Sign in with your Acme Corp SSO credentials (your @acmecorp.com email and password).

    Complete the MFA challenge (hardware key or authenticator app).

    Download and install the VPN client for your operating system from the enrollment page.

    Open the VPN client and sign in when prompted. The client will register your device automatically.

    The client performs a device posture check: OS version, disk encryption status, and endpoint agent. If any check fails, you will see a specific error message with remediation steps.

    Once all checks pass, the VPN connects. A shield icon in your system tray confirms an active connection.

  




  4. Daily Use

  The VPN client runs automatically at startup and connects when it detects you are outside the corporate network. You do not need to manually connect each day.

  To verify your connection status, click the VPN icon in your system tray. A green shield means connected; a gray shield means disconnected.

  Split tunneling is disabled for connections to production and management zones. Personal internet traffic (streaming, personal email) routes outside the VPN tunnel when accessing non-corporate destinations.





  5. Troubleshooting

  
    SymptomLikely CauseFix

    Client shows "Device posture check failed"Disk encryption disabled or endpoint agent not runningEnable FileVault/BitLocker and ensure the EDR agent is active. Restart the client.

    Client shows "Authentication failed"Expired password or MFA timeoutReset your password via the self-service portal. Re-enroll MFA if needed.

    Connected but cannot reach internal sitesDNS resolution issueDisconnect and reconnect. If the issue persists, run warp-cli diagnostics and send the output to IT.

    Slow connection speedsISP throttling or poor Wi-Fi signalTry a wired connection. If still slow, contact IT to check the nearest VPN edge node.

    Client crashes on startupOutdated client versionUninstall and reinstall from vpn.acmecorp.com/enroll.

  




  6. Support

  For VPN issues not resolved by the troubleshooting steps above, contact IT at it-support@acmecorp.com or Slack #it-help.
title: VPN Access Guide

text:
# Vulnerability Management Policy

**Document ID:** ITSEC-005
**Effective Date:** March 15, 2024
**Approved by:** VP of Engineering
**Classification:** Confidential

## 1. Purpose

This policy defines Acme Corp's approach to identifying, triaging, remediating, and tracking security vulnerabilities across all company systems. It ensures that vulnerabilities are addressed within defined timelines based on severity.

## 2. Vulnerability Sources

The InfoSec team monitors the following sources for new vulnerabilities:

- Automated vulnerability scans (weekly for production, monthly for corporate systems).
- CVE databases (NVD, MITRE) and vendor security advisories.
- Dependency scanning in CI/CD pipelines (Snyk, Dependabot).
- Penetration test findings (annual external, semi-annual internal).
- Bug bounty program submissions.
- Threat intelligence feeds.

## 3. CVE Triage Process

When a new CVE or vulnerability is identified, the InfoSec team triages it within 24 hours:

1. **Assess applicability:** Does the vulnerability affect software, libraries, or configurations in use at Acme Corp?
2. **Determine severity:** Use the CVSS v3.1 base score as the starting point, adjusted for Acme Corp's specific exposure (network accessibility, data sensitivity, compensating controls).
3. **Assign owner:** The system owner responsible for the affected component is assigned as the remediation owner.
4. **Set deadline:** Per the patch cadence in Section 4.
5. **Log in tracker:** The vulnerability is recorded in the vulnerability tracking system with CVE identifier, affected systems, severity, owner, and remediation deadline.

Vulnerabilities that are not applicable (e.g., affecting a component not deployed at Acme Corp) are marked as "Not Applicable" with a justification note.

## 4. Patch Cadence and Remediation SLAs

Security patches and remediations must be completed within the following timelines:

| Severity | CVSS Score | Remediation Deadline |
|---|---|---|
| Critical | 9.0–10.0 | 7 days |
| High | 7.0–8.9 | 30 days |
| Medium | 4.0–6.9 | Next monthly patch window |
| Low | 0.1–3.9 | Next quarterly patch cycle |

The remediation deadline is measured from the date the vulnerability is triaged, not the date it was publicly disclosed.

For critical vulnerabilities in production systems, the InfoSec team may deploy an emergency patch outside the standard change management process, with post-hoc review within 48 hours.

## 5. Exception Process

If a vulnerability cannot be remediated within the SLA (e.g., no patch available, business-critical system with a complex upgrade path), the system owner must submit a Security Exception Request:

- **Justification:** Why the remediation cannot be completed on time.
- **Compensating controls:** What measures are in place to reduce risk until the fix is applied (network isolation, WAF rules, monitoring, access restrictions).
- **Revised deadline:** A specific date by which the remediation will be completed.

Exceptions are approved by the InfoSec team lead and the VP of Engineering. Approved exceptions are reviewed every 90 days. The maximum exception duration is 180 days; after that, a new exception request is required.

## 6. Dependency Management

All application dependencies are tracked in a software bill of materials (SBOM). The CI/CD pipeline automatically flags dependencies with known vulnerabilities and blocks deployment of critical-severity findings.

Development teams are responsible for updating dependencies in their services. Dependencies with critical or high CVEs that are not updated within the SLA are escalated to the engineering manager.

## 7. Metrics and Reporting

The InfoSec team reports the following metrics monthly to the VP of Engineering:

- Total open vulnerabilities by severity.
- Mean time to remediate (MTTR) by severity.
- Number of overdue remediations.
- Number of active exceptions.
- Vulnerability scanning coverage (percentage of systems scanned on schedule).

Quarterly reports are provided to the Chief Compliance Officer for regulatory and audit purposes.

## 8. Policy Governance

This policy is reviewed annually by the Information Security team. Questions should be directed to security@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 2.1 — Last revised March 15, 2024*
title: Vulnerability Management Policy

# Warranty Terms

**Effective Date:** October 1, 2023
**Applies to:** All physical products sold or shipped by Acme Corp (hardware kits, developer devices, branded merchandise)

---

## 1. Standard Warranty

All physical products shipped by Acme Corp carry a 1-year standard warranty from the date of delivery. The standard warranty covers defects in materials and workmanship under normal use.

During the warranty period, Acme Corp will, at its discretion, repair or replace the defective product at no cost to the customer. Replacement products may be new or refurbished and carry the remainder of the original warranty period or 90 days, whichever is longer.

## 2. Extended Warranty

Customers may purchase an extended warranty at the time of order or within 30 days of delivery:

| Option | Duration | Cost |
|---|---|---|
| Extended 1-Year | 2 years total coverage | $49 |
| Extended 2-Year | 3 years total coverage | $89 |

Extended warranty terms are identical to the standard warranty. Coverage begins at the expiration of the standard warranty period.

Extended warranties are non-refundable once the standard warranty period has expired.

## 3. Claim Process

To file a warranty claim:

1. Contact operations@acmecorp.com with your order number, a description of the defect, and photos if applicable.
2. The Operations team reviews the claim and responds within 3 business days.
3. If approved, Acme Corp provides a prepaid shipping label for the defective product.
4. Upon receipt and inspection, a replacement is shipped within 5 business days.

For products under $50 in value, Acme Corp may ship a replacement without requiring return of the defective item.

## 4. What Is NOT Covered

The warranty does not cover:

- Damage from misuse, abuse, accident, or neglect.
- Damage from unauthorized modification or repair.
- Normal wear and tear, including cosmetic damage (scratches, dents) that does not affect functionality.
- Damage caused by use with non-compatible third-party accessories or power supplies.
- Products with removed or altered serial numbers.
- Software or firmware issues (covered under the applicable software license agreement, not this warranty).
- Consumable components (batteries, cables, stylus tips) after the first 90 days.
- Damage from exposure to liquids, extreme temperatures, or corrosive environments.
- Products purchased from unauthorized resellers.

## 5. Interaction with Refund Policy

The warranty and the Refund Policy operate independently:

- A subscription refund (governed by the Refund Policy) does not void an active warranty on physical products.
- A warranty replacement does not extend or restart the 30-day refund window for the subscription.
- Customers may have an active warranty claim and a subscription refund request at the same time. Each is processed under its own policy.

If a product is defective on arrival, the customer may choose either a warranty replacement (this document) or a full product refund under the Refund Policy's 30-day window. Both options cannot be exercised for the same item.

## 6. Limitation of Liability

Acme Corp's liability under this warranty is limited to the repair or replacement of the defective product. Acme Corp is not liable for indirect, incidental, or consequential damages arising from product defects, including but not limited to lost data, lost revenue, or costs of substitute equipment.

## 7. Policy Governance

This document is maintained by the Operations team. Questions should be directed to operations@acmecorp.com.

---

*Version 1.1 — Last revised October 1, 2023*
title: Warranty Terms

# Webhook Documentation

**Last Updated:** October 1, 2024

Webhooks allow your application to receive real-time notifications when events occur in Acme Corp. Instead of polling the API, register a webhook endpoint and Acme Corp will push events to you as they happen.

## 1. Supported Events

| Event | Trigger |
|---|---|
| `record.created` | A new record is created |
| `record.updated` | A record's content, tags, or metadata is modified |
| `record.deleted` | A record is soft-deleted |
| `record.restored` | A previously deleted record is restored |
| `user.invited` | A new user invitation is sent |
| `user.removed` | A user is removed from the account |
| `user.role_changed` | A user's role is updated |
| `export.completed` | A bulk data export finishes processing |

Subscribe to specific events when registering a webhook, or use `*` to receive all events (not recommended for high-volume accounts).

## 2. Payload Structure

Every webhook delivery is an HTTP `POST` with a JSON body:

```json
{
  "id": "evt_m3n4o5p6",
  "type": "record.updated",
  "created_at": "2025-01-15T10:40:00Z",
  "account_id": "acct_a1b2c3",
  "data": {
    "record_id": "rec_x7y8z9",
    "changes": ["content", "tags"],
    "updated_by": "user_d4e5f6"
  }
}
```

The `data` field varies by event type. Each event type's schema is documented in the API Reference Guide under the Webhooks section.

## 3. Signature Verification

Every delivery includes an `X-Acme-Signature` header containing an HMAC-SHA256 signature of the raw request body, computed using your webhook secret:

```
X-Acme-Signature: sha256=a1b2c3d4e5f6...
```

To verify:

1. Read the raw request body (before JSON parsing).
2. Compute HMAC-SHA256 of the body using your webhook secret as the key.
3. Compare the computed signature to the value in `X-Acme-Signature` using a constant-time comparison function.
4. Reject the request if the signatures do not match.

Never skip signature verification in production. Without it, any party that knows your endpoint URL can send forged events.

## 4. Retry Behavior

If your endpoint does not respond with a `2xx` status code within 10 seconds, the delivery is retried with exponential backoff:

| Attempt | Delay After Previous Attempt |
|---|---|
| 1 (initial) | Immediate |
| 2 | 10 seconds |
| 3 | 30 seconds |
| 4 | 90 seconds |
| 5 | 270 seconds (~4.5 min) |
| 6 (final) | 810 seconds (~13.5 min) |

After 6 failed attempts (approximately 20 minutes total), the delivery is marked as failed. If a webhook accumulates 50 consecutive failed deliveries, it is automatically disabled and an email notification is sent to the account owner.

## 5. Idempotency

Your endpoint may receive the same event more than once (due to retries or rare internal duplication). Use the `id` field in the payload to deduplicate. If you have already processed an event with a given ID, return `200 OK` without reprocessing.

## 6. Managing Webhooks

- **Register:** `POST /v2/webhooks` (see the API Reference Guide).
- **List:** `GET /v2/webhooks` — returns all active webhooks.
- **Delete:** `DELETE /v2/webhooks/{webhook_id}` — removes the subscription and cancels pending deliveries.
- **Delivery log:** Available in the admin console under **Settings > API > Webhooks > Delivery Log**. Each entry shows the event, delivery status, HTTP response code, latency, and retry history.

## 7. Best Practices

- Respond quickly. Process the event asynchronously (queue it) and return `200 OK` within 1–2 seconds. Long-running processing during the request risks timeouts.
- Use signature verification. Always.
- Implement idempotency. Retries are normal.
- Monitor your delivery log. A rising failure rate usually means your endpoint is unhealthy.
- Use HTTPS. Webhook URLs must use `https://`. Plain HTTP endpoints are rejected at registration.

---

*Questions about webhooks: api-support@acmecorp.com or the API Reference Guide.*
title: Webhook Documentation

Wellness Program Overview
  



Wellness Program Overview


Effective Date: June 1, 2024
Applies to: All full-time Acme Corp employees



  1. Gym Reimbursement

  Acme Corp reimburses up to $50 per month toward gym memberships or fitness classes. Eligible expenses include:

  
    Monthly gym or fitness studio memberships.

    Group fitness classes (yoga, cycling, martial arts, etc.).

    Virtual fitness subscriptions.

  
  Reimbursement requests are submitted through the benefits portal with a receipt or membership statement. Requests must be submitted within 60 days of the charge. Unused monthly amounts do not roll over.





  2. Annual Health Screening

  All full-time employees are eligible for one company-sponsored health screening per calendar year at no cost. The screening includes:

  
    Biometric measurements (blood pressure, BMI, waist circumference).

    Blood panel (cholesterol, glucose, triglycerides).

    Confidential results report sent directly to the employee.

  
  Screenings are offered on-site during Q1 each year. Employees who cannot attend the on-site event may complete a screening at a participating provider and submit the results for credit. Scheduling details are communicated via company-wide email in January.





  3. Employee Assistance Program (EAP)

  Acme Corp provides access to a confidential Employee Assistance Program through ComPsych at no cost to employees or their household members. The EAP offers:

  
    Up to 6 confidential counseling sessions per issue per year (in-person or virtual).

    24/7 crisis support hotline.

    Referrals for legal, financial, and work-life resources.

    Substance abuse assessment and referral.

  
  EAP usage is completely confidential. No information is shared with Acme Corp management or Human Resources. Contact ComPsych directly at 1-800-555-0199 or through the online portal at eap.acmecorp.com.





  4. Wellness Incentives

  Employees who complete all three wellness activities during the calendar year — gym reimbursement participation, annual health screening, and an EAP financial wellness webinar — receive a $200 wellness credit applied to their December paycheck.





  5. Contact

  The wellness program is administered by Human Resources. Questions should be directed to benefits@acmecorp.com or ext. 4200.
title: Wellness Program Overview
